title: Bump markdown from 3.6 to 3.8.1
body:
Bumps [markdown](https://github.com/Python-Markdown/markdown) from 3.6 to 3.8.1.
Release notes

Sourced from markdown's releases.

Release 3.8.1

Fixed

  • Ensure incomplete markup declaration in raw HTML doesn't crash parser (#1534).
  • Fixed dropped content in md_in_html (#1526).
  • Fixed HTML handling corner case that prevented some content from not being rendered (#1528).

Release 3.8

Changed

  • DRY fix in abbr extension by introducing method create_element (#1483).
  • Clean up test directory by removing some redundant tests and port non-redundant cases to the newer test framework.
  • Improved performance of the raw HTML post-processor (#1510).

Fixed

  • Backslash Unescape IDs set via attr_list on toc (#1493).
  • Ensure md_in_html processes content inside "markdown" blocks as they are parsed outside of "markdown" blocks to keep things more consistent for third-party extensions (#1503).
  • md_in_html handle tags within inline code blocks better (#1075).
  • md_in_html fix handling of one-liner block HTML handling (#1074).
  • Ensure <center> is treated like a block-level element (#1481).
  • Ensure that abbr extension respects AtomicString and does not process perceived abbreviations in these strings (#1512).
  • Ensure smarty extension correctly renders nested closing quotes (#1514).

Release 3.7

Changed

Refactor abbr Extension

A new AbbrTreeprocessor has been introduced, which replaces the now deprecated AbbrInlineProcessor. Abbreviation processing now happens after Attribute Lists, avoiding a conflict between the two extensions (#1460).

The AbbrPreprocessor class has been renamed to AbbrBlockprocessor, which better reflects what it is. AbbrPreprocessor has been deprecated.

A call to Markdown.reset() now clears all previously defined abbreviations.

Abbreviations are now sorted by length before executing AbbrTreeprocessor to ensure that multi-word abbreviations are implemented even if an abbreviation exists for one of those component words. (#1465)

Abbreviations without a definition are now ignored. This avoids applying abbr tags to text without a title value.

Added an optional glossary configuration option to the abbreviations extension.

... (truncated)

Changelog

Sourced from markdown's changelog.

[3.8.1] - 2025-06-18

Fixed

  • Ensure incomplete markup declaration in raw HTML doesn't crash parser (#1534).
  • Fixed dropped content in md_in_html (#1526).
  • Fixed HTML handling corner case that prevented some content from not being rendered (#1528).

[3.8.0] - 2025-04-09

Changed

  • DRY fix in abbr extension by introducing method create_element (#1483).
  • Clean up test directory by removing some redundant tests and port non-redundant cases to the newer test framework.
  • Improved performance of the raw HTML post-processor (#1510).

Fixed

  • Backslash Unescape IDs set via attr_list on toc (#1493).
  • Ensure md_in_html processes content inside "markdown" blocks as they are parsed outside of "markdown" blocks to keep things more consistent for third-party extensions (#1503).
  • md_in_html handle tags within inline code blocks better (#1075).
  • md_in_html fix handling of one-liner block HTML handling (#1074).
  • Ensure <center> is treated like a block-level element (#1481).
  • Ensure that abbr extension respects AtomicString and does not process perceived abbreviations in these strings (#1512).
  • Ensure smarty extension correctly renders nested closing quotes (#1514).

[3.7.0] - 2024-08-16

Changed

  • Refactor abbr Extension

    A new AbbrTreeprocessor has been introduced, which replaces the now deprecated AbbrInlineProcessor. Abbreviation processing now happens after Attribute Lists, avoiding a conflict between the two extensions (#1460).

    The AbbrPreprocessor class has been renamed to AbbrBlockprocessor, which better reflects what it is. AbbrPreprocessor has been deprecated.

    A call to Markdown.reset() now clears all previously defined abbreviations.

    Abbreviations are now sorted by length before executing AbbrTreeprocessor to ensure that multi-word abbreviations are implemented even if an abbreviation exists for one of those component words. (#1465)

    Abbreviations without a definition are now ignored. This avoids applying

... (truncated)

Commits
  • 3870f20 Bump version to 3.8.1
  • 8207214 Ensure incomplete markup declaration in raw HTML doesn't crash parser.
  • 64a3c0f Fix HTML handling of \</>
  • f2b9fd1 Ensure md_in_html does not drop content
  • 513de8a Update pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish to v1.12.4
  • b34e1d0 Bump version to 3.8
  • e6b7163 Update deploy workflow to normalize version
  • bd67d48 Improve changelog validation
  • e912575 Fix incorrect TOC list structure in docs
  • 42d4b43 Fix CI badge in README

title: simplify-architecture: collapse daemon into plugin, drop systemd
body:
## Summary

Follow-up to [PR #1](https://github.com/MaxInertia/claude-voice-input-channel/pull/1) (review-fixes). That PR added a systemd layer to manage the separate `voice-sttd` and `voice-stt-ptt` services. This PR deletes that whole layer ŌĆö the daemon is now spawned as a Python subprocess of the TS channel plugin, tied to the Claude Code session lifetime.

The multi-session VRAM argument that justified the three-process architecture turned out to be a phantom requirement (we confirmed this in conversation: one user, one mic, one PTT button, never two sessions competing for voice). Collapsing trades a small model-reload cost (~2ŌĆō3 seconds on each fresh `claude` launch) for a dramatically simpler install flow that matches voicemode's 3-command ergonomics:

```
/plugin marketplace add MaxInertia/claude-voice-input-channel
/plugin install voice-stt@voice-stt-local
/voice-stt:install
```

Net LOC delta: **+1,750 / ŌłÆ1,285 Ōēł +465**, but 663 of those added lines are the new design plan document itself. Subtracting the plan, the actual code+docs change is **~ŌłÆ200 net** ŌĆö the refactor is strictly deletion-heavy.

## Phases (each commit is self-contained)

| # | Commit | Change |
|---|---|---|
| plan | `d1568fe` | Write the design plan, with feedback from plannotator |
| 1 | `32be8d7` | `daemon.py` speaks stdio: start/stop on stdin, transcripts on stdout, logs on stderr. Delete ctrl/out socket serving code. |
| 2 | `64d19f0` | PTT listener becomes a thread inside `voice-sttd`. Direct method calls to `start_recording()`/`stop_recording()`, no IPC. |
| 3 | `19a809f` | `src/voice_stt/config.py` loads `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/voice-stt/config`. Shell env wins over config file. `plugin/config.template` ships as the bundled default. |
| 4 | `66f3861` | `plugin/channel/voice-stt-channel.ts` rewritten to `Bun.spawn('uv', 'run', 'voice-sttd')` and forward stdoutŌåÆ`notifications/claude/channel`. `src/voice_stt/singleton.py` uses `fcntl.flock` on `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/voice-stt/daemon.lock` to prevent concurrent instances, with exit code 2 as a permanent-contention sentinel that bypasses the TS restart loop. |
| 5 | `cf9485b` | `plugin/commands/install.md` ŌĆö `/voice-stt:install` slash command that runs prereq checks, `uv sync`, `bun install`, creates the XDG config from template, migrates legacy `.env` and systemd units if detected. |
| 6 | `6c5b5b7` | Delete obsolete plumbing: `scripts/voice-stt-svc`, `scripts/claude-voice`, `scripts/install-systemd-units`, `scripts/systemd/`, `scripts/xbindkeysrc.example`, `src/voice_stt/client.py`, `src/voice_stt/ptt_listener.py`, `.env.example`. `scripts/` directory gone entirely. |
| 7 | `83ea0f9` | README rewritten around the new 3-command install. Old plan doc gets a retrospective pointer. Obsidian vault setup doc updated with the new architecture diagram. |

## What's preserved

The architectural cleanups are limited to the lifecycle/packaging layer. All the correctness work from PR #1 is unchanged:

- **Segment join** (one PTT press = one transcript = one channel event)
- **PTT rescan-on-device-disappearance** (survives input-remapper reload)
- **Broadcast lock scoping** (though `_broadcast` itself is gone ŌĆö transcripts go straight to stdout now)
- **Background transcription worker** (still serialized, still bounded queue, still drops oldest on overflow)
- **Persistent `sd.InputStream`** (prevents the open/close race and the virtual-source alternating-zero bug)
- **XDG_RUNTIME_DIR** usage ŌĆö now just for the singleton lockfile, no longer for sockets
- **`_bridge_pulse_source`** ŌĆö still bridges `VOICE_STT_PULSE_SOURCE` ŌåÆ `PULSE_SOURCE` before PortAudio opens the pulse backend, now runs after `config.load()` instead of being driven by `voice-stt-svc`'s bash
- **Prompt-injection caveats** in docs
- **Hardware PTT wiring** via input-remapper ŌåÆ `KEY_F20` ŌĆö unchanged

## Testing

Smoke tests run autonomously during development (systemd legacy services were stopped for each test so the mic and singleton lock were free):

### Phase 1: daemon over stdio
```
$ timeout 10 bash -c '(sleep 3; echo) | uv run voice-sttd 2>&1'
[voice-sttd] loading model=medium.en device=cuda compute=float16
[voice-sttd] model ready
[voice-sttd] opening input device: 'pulse' (PULSE_SOURCE='<unset>')
[voice-sttd] audio input stream open (persistent)
[voice-sttd] ready; awaiting stdin commands
[voice-sttd] bye
```

### Phase 2: PTT thread inside daemon
```
[voice-sttd] PTT: listening for KEY_F20 on 6 devices
[voice-sttd] PTT:   - /dev/input/event12  input-remapper Razer Razer Naga V2 HyperSpeed forwarded
[voice-sttd] PTT:   - /dev/input/event2  input-remapper keyboard
...
```
Thread spins up, enumerates all 6 watched devices via `_find_keyboards`, same code path as the old standalone `voice-stt-ptt`.

### Phase 3: XDG config loaded and honored
```
$ printf 'VOICE_STT_MODEL=small.en\nVOICE_STT_PULSE_SOURCE=alsa_input.pci-0000_09_00.4.analog-stereo\n' > /tmp/xdg-test/voice-stt/config
$ XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/tmp/xdg-test timeout 10 bash -c '(sleep 3; echo) | uv run voice-sttd 2>&1'
[voice-sttd] loaded 2 setting(s) from /tmp/xdg-test/voice-stt/config
[voice-sttd] loading model=small.en device=cuda compute=float16
[voice-sttd] opening input device: 'pulse' (PULSE_SOURCE='alsa_input.pci-0000_09_00.4.analog-stereo')
```
Model override honored, PULSE_SOURCE bridged post-config-load.

### Phase 4: singleton lock
```
$ ( (sleep 5; echo) | uv run voice-sttd 2>&1 ) &
$ sleep 3.5; echo | uv run voice-sttd 2>&1; echo "second exit=$?"
[voice-sttd] another voice-stt daemon is already running (lock held on
/run/user/1000/voice-stt/daemon.lock, previous pid: 306038).
second exit=2
```
First daemon holds the lock; second exits with code 2 immediately. Lockfile content is the holding pid for diagnostics.

### Phase 6+7: post-deletion full lifecycle
With everything deleted and the real `~/.config/voice-stt/config` in place (migrated from the repo-root `.env`):
```
$ timeout 10 bash -c '(sleep 4; echo) | uv run voice-sttd 2>&1 | grep -E "loaded|loading|opening|PTT: listening"'
[voice-sttd] loaded 6 setting(s) from /home/maxinertia/.config/voice-stt/config
[voice-sttd] loading model=medium.en device=cuda compute=float16
[voice-sttd] opening input device: 'pulse' (PULSE_SOURCE='alsa_input.pci-0000_09_00.4.analog-stereo')
[voice-sttd] PTT: listening for KEY_F20 on 6 devices
```
Config loaded from XDG path, model loaded, PULSE_SOURCE bridged, PTT thread online, clean shutdown on EOF.

## What I couldn't test autonomously

The Bun channel plugin's `Bun.spawn` ŌåÆ Python path, the full TSŌåÆPython stdio IPC, the restart-on-crash loop, and the `/voice-stt:install` slash command all require a fresh Claude Code session because:

1. The currently-running Bun MCP server (pid 264531) has the OLD cached channel code baked into memory ŌĆö it's still reconnecting to the `/run/user/1000/voice-stt/out.sock` path that no longer exists
2. Refreshing the plugin cache doesn't restart the long-lived in-memory Bun process
3. `/voice-stt:install` only runs inside a Claude Code session, and running it in the CURRENT session would interrupt the work

**User needs to do after merging:**

```bash
# 1. Exit the current Claude Code session
#    (the running Bun plugin process will die)

# 2. In a fresh shell / fresh Claude Code launch, refresh the plugin cache:
/plugin marketplace update voice-stt-local
/plugin uninstall voice-stt@voice-stt-local
/plugin install voice-stt@voice-stt-local
/reload-plugins

# 3. Exit that session and relaunch with the channel flag:
claude --dangerously-load-development-channels plugin:voice-stt@voice-stt-local

# 4. Hold Naga `=` and dictate a test phrase
#    - Expect: <channel source="voice-stt" seq="1"> arrives within 2-3s
#    - Expect: no 'Noise Canceling source' alternating-silence regression
#    - Expect: /exit kills the Python child within ~2s (watch pgrep)
```

If anything in step 4 fails, check `~/.claude/debug/<session-id>.txt` for the channel server's stderr (which now forwards the Python daemon's stderr verbatim).

## Migration notes

- **Legacy systemd user units** from PR #1 are still enabled on the dev machine. `/voice-stt:install` detects them and offers to `systemctl --user disable --now` + remove the unit files. They can also be cleaned up manually.
- **The repo-root `.env`** from PR #1 was already migrated to `~/.config/voice-stt/config` during Phase 8 testing (the one `VOICE_STT_PULSE_SOURCE` override for bypassing the Noise Canceling source). The original `.env` file has been deleted from the working tree (was gitignored, not tracked).
- **The stale plugin cache** at `~/.claude/plugins/cache/voice-stt-local/` will auto-refresh on the next `/plugin install`.
- **No rollback plan** since PR #1 is already on master ŌĆö if this PR needs to be reverted, `git revert` the merge commit and re-run `install-systemd-units` from the reverted tree. All the systemd infrastructure is in PR #1's history.

## Post-Deploy Monitoring & Validation

- **What to monitor** ŌĆö `journalctl --user -u voice-sttd.service -f` before the branch merges (confirm the legacy services are cleanly stopped post-migration), `~/.claude/debug/<session-id>.txt` after the branch merges (all daemon logging lands there via the TS channel's stderr forwarder)
- **Validation checks** ŌĆö
  - `systemctl --user is-active voice-sttd.service voice-stt-ptt.service` ŌåÆ should return `inactive inactive` after cleanup (or `Unit ... not found` after the unit files are deleted)
  - `pgrep -af 'voice-sttd|voice-stt-ptt'` during an active Claude Code session ŌåÆ should show exactly one Python process, child of a Bun process
  - `pgrep -af 'voice-sttd'` after `/exit` from Claude Code ŌåÆ should return nothing within 2 seconds
  - `ls -la $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/voice-stt/daemon.lock` ŌåÆ should exist only while a session is active
- **Expected healthy behavior** ŌĆö Hold Naga `=`, speak, release ŌåÆ one `<channel source="voice-stt" seq="N">` event arrives in the session within ~1 second of release
- **Failure signal / rollback trigger** ŌĆö If dictation arrives as alternating silent buffers OR if the daemon fails to start cleanly from the plugin path, investigate the PULSE_SOURCE config in `~/.config/voice-stt/config` first, then the debug log. Rollback is `git revert` the PR.
- **Validation window & owner** ŌĆö First 3 days of daily use. If no issues by then, consider the refactor landed.

---

title: feat: development environment configuration (P1-E1-F3)
body:
## Summary
- Extends `docker-compose.override.yml` with 5 application service stubs (auth, glucose, fitness, nutrition, ml-insights) featuring hot-reload via air (Go) and uvicorn --reload (Python)
- Exposes debug ports (40001-40005) bound to 127.0.0.1 for delve (Go) and debugpy (Python) IDE attachment
- Adds development seed data for PostgreSQL (users, families, foods, meals, recipes) and TimescaleDB (7 days of glucose readings, fitness metrics, workouts, sleep sessions, Dexcom events)
- Configures Compose profiles (`dev`) to isolate dev services from production; relaxes resource limits (256MB Go, 1GB/4GB Python, no CPU caps)
- Provides `.air.toml` shared hot-reload config and `.vscode/launch.json` debug attach configurations
- Creates service placeholder directories for future application code

## Dependencies
- P1-E1-F1-docker-compose-datastores (PR #1, merged into feature/phase1-foundation)

## Key Design Decisions
- Application services are stubs that gracefully fall through to `tail -f /dev/null` until actual code exists
- Seed data uses fixed UUIDs for cross-database referential integrity (PostgreSQL user IDs referenced in TimescaleDB readings)
- All seed data is idempotent via `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` (PostgreSQL) and existence checks (TimescaleDB)
- Go images use `golang:1.23` (latest stable); Python uses `python:3.14-rc-slim` -- to be updated when Go 1.26 and Python 3.14 stable are released

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify `docker compose config` succeeds with merged base + override
- [ ] Verify `docker compose --profile dev up` starts all data stores and app service stubs
- [ ] Verify debug ports are accessible on localhost (telnet 127.0.0.1 40001-40005)
- [ ] Verify `docker compose -f docker-compose.yml up` does NOT expose debug ports or start app services
- [ ] Verify seed data populates on first database init (query users, glucose_readings tables)
- [ ] Verify VS Code can attach to service debug ports using launch.json configurations
- [ ] Verify re-running seed scripts is idempotent (no duplicate data)

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Setup Admin API and Type API
feat: build cyclonedds 0.10.5 from source, support Python 3.10+
body:
Build cyclonedds 0.10.5 from source during install, removing the dependency on third-party pre-built wheels. The cyclonedds version is locked to 0.10.5, matching the official unitree SDK.

## Changes

- **cyclonedds C library** (`thirdparty/cyclonedds/`) and **Python bindings** (`thirdparty/cyclonedds-python/`) added as git submodules (tag 0.10.5)
- Custom `setup.py` with `build_ext` that automatically:
  1. Finds C source from submodule, or downloads 0.10.5 tarball from GitHub as fallback
  2. Finds Python bindings from submodule, or downloads sdist from PyPI as fallback
  3. Builds C library with cmake
  4. Compiles `_clayer` Python C extension
  5. Bundles `libddsc.so` into the wheel
- `cyclonedds` is no longer a pip dependency ŌĆö it's built from source and bundled into the package
- `requires-python` lowered from `>=3.11` to `>=3.10`
- Removed dependency on carlosdp's custom pre-built wheels

## Usage in other projects

```toml
[project]
dependencies = ["unitree-sdk2py"]

[tool.uv.sources]
unitree-sdk2py = { git = "https://github.com/MiaoDX-fork-and-pruning/unitree_sdk2_python_uv" }
```

`uv sync` automatically downloads and builds cyclonedds from source ŌĆö no extra steps needed.

## Prerequisites

- cmake, C compiler (gcc/clang)
- Python >= 3.10

title: Implement Week 2 synthetic generation, runner workflow, and golden sets
body:
## Summary
This PR implements **Week 2 (Days 6-10)** from `docs/01-conversion-validator-spec.md`:

- Day 6: synthetic expression generation
- Day 7: synthetic pipeline generation
- Day 8: ground-truth suite generation + converter evaluation
- Day 9: runner workflow for 50-100 synthetic pipeline evaluation + CCS distribution + triage
- Day 10: golden set materialization (`golden_sets/expressions.json`, `golden_sets/pipelines.json`)

It also includes alignment fixes so synthetic expected outputs match current `wkmigrate` emitter behavior exactly.

## Scope
### New Week 2 core modules
- `src/lakeflow_migration_validator/synthetic/expression_generator.py`
- `src/lakeflow_migration_validator/synthetic/pipeline_generator.py`
- `src/lakeflow_migration_validator/synthetic/ground_truth.py`
- `src/lakeflow_migration_validator/synthetic/runner.py`
- `src/lakeflow_migration_validator/golden_set.py`
- `src/lakeflow_migration_validator/report.py` (report/distribution support)

### New tests
- `tests/unit/synthetic/test_expression_generator.py`
- `tests/unit/synthetic/test_pipeline_generator.py`
- `tests/unit/synthetic/test_ground_truth.py`
- `tests/unit/synthetic/test_runner.py`
- `tests/unit/synthetic/test_golden_set.py`
- `tests/unit/synthetic/test_day9_day10_workflow.py`
- `tests/unit/synthetic/__init__.py`

### Generated artifacts
- `golden_sets/expressions.json`
- `golden_sets/pipelines.json`

## wkmigrate alignment fixes included
### Expression templates
Updated all expected Python outputs to match current emitter output format from `wkmigrate.parsers.expression_functions` and expression emitter tests, including:

- `concat` as `str(a) + str(b)`
- string funcs (`toUpper`, `toLower`, `trim`, `substring`, `replace`, `indexOf`, `startsWith`, `endsWith`, `contains`)
- math/logical operators with expected parenthesization
- `div` as `int(a / b)`
- casts (`int`, `float`, `string`, `bool`)
- collection funcs (`first`, `last`, `coalesce`, `empty`)
- datetime helpers (`utcNow`, `formatDateTime`)
- removed unsupported `dayOfMonth`

Added compile-validity test for all generated `expected_python` expressions.

### Pipeline activity shape
Updated default synthetic activity types to:

- `SetVariable`, `IfCondition`, `DatabricksNotebook`, `Copy`, `Lookup`, `WebActivity`, `ForEach`

and removed `AppendVariable` from defaults.

Generated activity payloads now follow `wkmigrate`'s normalized translator input shape (snake_case keys and top-level `IfCondition.expression`).

### Mode stubs
`PipelineGenerator` now fails fast for unsupported generation modes without provider:

- raises `NotImplementedError("LLM mode requires a judge_provider")` when mode is `llm` or `adversarial` and provider is absent.

## Behavioral highlights
- `GroundTruthSuite.evaluate_converter(...)` runs converter output through `evaluate(...)` and returns aggregate `Report`.
- `run_synthetic_workflow(...)` returns:
  - aggregated `Report`
  - CCS distribution summary
  - triaged failure list (threshold + expression mismatch reasons)
- `materialize_golden_sets(...)` writes expression + pipeline golden artifacts and loaders round-trip them.

## Tests
Executed:

- `PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit/synthetic -q`
- `PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -q`
- `PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v`

Result:
- **90 passed, 13 skipped**

## Review guide
1. Review `synthetic/expression_generator.py` first (template correctness vs emitter outputs).
2. Review `synthetic/pipeline_generator.py` for normalized activity JSON and mode guards.
3. Review `synthetic/ground_truth.py` and `synthetic/runner.py` for evaluation/triage flow.
4. Review `golden_set.py` + generated `golden_sets/*.json` for artifact format and loadability.
5. Review `tests/unit/synthetic/*` as acceptance criteria.

## Notes
- `golden_sets/pipelines.json` is large by design (materialized Week 2 Day 10 artifact).

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Low risk because this PR only adds a static golden-set JSON file and does not change runtime code paths.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds `golden_sets/expressions.json`, a versioned golden-set artifact with **200** categorized ADF expressions and their `expected_python` translations (string/math/datetime/logical/collection/nested) for use in validation/regression testing.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 2bc04c9b9013b64b96526fae05bc2a5ce4e64924. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

title: WP8: Sweep 1 census scripts (blocked on API key)
body:
## Summary

- Add `scripts/sweep1_census.sh`: master orchestration script that queries all 24 models (3 runs each) in cheapest-first order with a $10 budget cap, then extracts CSVs and evaluates against the reference
- Add `scripts/query_padme.py`: query 8 local Ollama models on Padme (free, no API cost)
- Add `scripts/summarize_sweep.py`: aggregate per-run metrics into a summary CSV with median F1, coverage, precision, cost, and latency per model

## Blocker

**Execution blocked** ŌĆö two issues prevent running the actual queries:
1. **OpenRouter API key expired**: `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in `~/.bashrc` returns HTTP 401 ("User not found")
2. **Padme Ollama offline**: port 11434 on padme times out (host reachable but service not running)

Scripts are tested with `--dry-run` and ready to execute once credentials are renewed.

## To run once unblocked

```bash
# Renew OpenRouter key, then:
eval "$(grep OPENROUTER ~/.bashrc)"
cd /path/to/aedist
bash scripts/sweep1_census.sh

# For Padme local models:
# Start Ollama on padme, then:
uv run python scripts/query_padme.py --prompt prompts/prompt_structured.txt --output outputs/sweep1_census/ --repeat 3

# Summarize:
uv run python scripts/summarize_sweep.py --metrics results/sweep1_census/all_metrics.json --queries outputs/sweep1_census/ --output results/sweep1_census/summary.csv
```

## Test plan

- [x] `sweep1_census.sh --dry-run` lists all 24 models x 3 runs in cost order
- [x] `query.py --dry-run` confirms 72 queries planned
- [ ] Run with valid API key (pending key renewal)
- [ ] Verify extracted CSVs match expected format
- [ ] Verify summary.csv has all models ranked by F1

  • The SSLContext caching feature originally introduced in 2.32.0 has created a new class of issues in Requests that have had negative impact across a number of use cases. The Requests team has decided to revert this feature as long term maintenance of it is proving to be unsustainable in its current iteration.

Deprecations

  • Added support for Python 3.14.
  • Dropped support for Python 3.8 following its end of support.

Security

title: Blender 5.1/Python 3.13 compatibility, profile migration, vendoring improvements, and Poppy Playtime profiles
body:
### Motivation
- Add compatibility with newer Blender releases (Blender 5.1 / Python 3.13) and make profile storage cross-platform and robust.
- Improve packaging/build reliability by vendoring already-installed distributions when `pip` installation into the distribution target fails.
- Support Blender's deferred property annotations so class auto-registration works with both legacy and newer annotation styles.
- Provide convenient, selectable generic game profiles for Poppy Playtime Chapters 1ŌĆō5 in the add-on UI.

### Description
- Updated `README.md` and `docs/source/installation.rst` to document Blender 5.1 / Python 3.13 support and cross-platform profile storage behavior.
- Extended `build.py` with `_iter_requirements`, `_requirement_import_name`, and `_copy_installed_distribution` helper functions and changed the requirements installation loop to create `umodel_tools/third_party`, attempt `pip install` per requirement, and fall back to copying an already-installed distribution when pip fails.
- Added support in `umodel_tools/auto_load.py` to detect Blender's deferred property wrapper (`_PropertyDeferred`) and extract `PointerProperty`/`CollectionProperty` dependencies so registration ordering works with deferred annotations.
- Added five generic shim game profiles under `umodel_tools/game_profiles/` for `poppy_playtime_chapter_1` through `poppy_playtime_chapter_5` that reuse the existing `generic` handlers and provide `GAME_NAME`/`GAME_DESCRIPTION` entries.
- Changed `umodel_tools/preferences.py` to store profiles in Blender's per-user config directory via `bpy.utils.user_resource('CONFIG', ...)`, added legacy `Documents/XModel` support and automatic migration via `_migrate_legacy_profiles`, made profile loading search both new and legacy locations with deduplication, and adjusted parsing/attribute assignment accordingly.
- Minor metadata update in `umodel_tools/__init__.py` to add a `doc_url` to `bl_info`.

### Testing
- No automated tests were added or executed as part of this change.

------

title: Python developer 2026 03 (part 2)
chore: remove broken python-package-conda.yml workflow
body:
## Summary

- The `python-package-conda.yml` workflow has been causing `startup_failure` on every push to `main` since at least March 2026
- The workflow file still exists on the `main` branch even though it was never functional
- Removing it to stop the continuous `startup_failure` noise in Actions

## Root Cause

The workflow was broken from the start for three reasons:

1. **Missing `environment.yml`** ŌĆö `conda env update --file environment.yml` fails immediately; this file was never added to the repo
2. **Broken `$CONDA` path injection** ŌĆö relies on `$CONDA` env var being set, but without `conda-incubator/setup-miniconda`, this variable is not available on GitHub-hosted runners
3. **Outdated action** ŌĆö uses `actions/setup-python@v3` (deprecated; current is `@v5`)

## Result

Every push to `main` triggered a `startup_failure` run (6 documented runs from 2026-02-18 to 2026-03-11). The workflow was never going to pass without significant rework, and the project does not use conda (it uses `requirements.txt`).

## Fix

Delete the file. The project's real CI (if needed) should use `pip` + `requirements.txt`, not conda.

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify no new `python-package-conda.yml` runs appear in Actions after this merges
- [ ] Confirm `python-publish.yml` is unaffected (it remains in place)

title: Migrate to llama-server and enhance AI capability handling
body:
This pull request introduces robust AI capability checks and error handling throughout the file organizing pipeline, ensuring that the backend gracefully handles cases where AI services (such as LLM or embeddings) are unavailable. It also updates documentation and environment configuration to reflect the switch from in-process `llama-cpp-python` to an external `llama-server` for LLM inference. The changes are grouped below by theme.

**AI Capability Checks and Error Handling**

* Added `_collect_organize_ai_errors` and `_build_ai_unavailable_result` helper functions to centralize AI availability checks and error reporting. These functions ensure that any AI-related failures are consistently logged and surfaced in API responses.
* Integrated live AI capability checks at multiple steps in the organize pipeline (classification, summary, rename, and final classification), returning a structured error response if any AI service is unavailable. This prevents partial or misleading results when AI is down. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR704-R722) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR733-R746) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cL681-R814) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR835-R848) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR874-R887) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR939-R952)
* Updated pipeline step numbering and ensured that AI status and timings are tracked in the pipeline metadata for each file processed. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR537) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cL692-R825) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cL718-R865) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cL743-R904) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cL764-R925) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cL796-R971)

**Service and Import Refactoring**

* Refactored service imports to clarify module boundaries, moving AI-related services under `app.services.ai` and file-related services under `app.services.files`. This improves code organization and maintainability. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cL51-R61) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR36-R39)

**Documentation and Environment Updates**

* Updated `.env.example` to remove in-process llama-cpp settings and add external `llama-server` configuration, reflecting the new runtime architecture.
* Revised `README.md` to explain the use of `llama-server`, update model instructions, clarify environment variables, and remove references to deprecated endpoints and files. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5R6-R14) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L22-R26) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L36-R54) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L81-R86) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L104-R114) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L134)

**Deprecation and Cleanup**

* Removed references to the deprecated `init_base_path.py` endpoint and related documentation, aligning with the new organize flow.

**Pipeline Robustness**

* Ensured that AI capability errors are surfaced early and consistently, preventing downstream failures and improving reliability for users when AI services are unavailable. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR181-R254) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR704-R722) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR733-R746) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cL681-R814) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR835-R848) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR874-R887) [[7]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR939-R952)

title: refactor: drop stopwords
body:
## Summary

- `_match_lang` raises `ValueError` when the closest available locale exceeds distance 10, instead of silently returning `None`. Uses `100000` as the initial `best_dist` sentinel so the loop always finds the true minimum before applying the threshold.
- Stopwords removed entirely ŌĆö they had no unit tests justifying their existence and were papering over a flaw in the double-negative detector.
- Double-negative detection replaced with a proximity regex allowing at most one intervening token (e.g. `"not a lie"`, `"n├Żo ├® mentira"`), fixing the underlying issue that stopwords were working around.
- `test_unsupported_language` updated to expect `ValueError`.

## Test plan

- [ ] `python -m pytest tests/` ŌĆö 11 passed, 111 subtests

title: Convert all Python files from Python 2 to Python 3
body: ## Summary Mechanical Python 2 ŌåÆ Python 3 migration across all three `.py` files. No logic or behavior changes. **Changes by file:** - **Container.py** ŌĆö `print` statements ŌåÆ `print()` calls; `push_file` opens in `'rb'` mode and decodes base64 output to `str` (required by Py3's `base64.b64encode` which expects `bytes`) - **HPC_data_collector.py** ŌĆö `print` statements ŌåÆ `print()` calls; bare `except:` ŌåÆ `except Exception:` (no longer silently swallows `KeyboardInterrupt`/`SystemExit`) - **DataParser.py** ŌĆö wraps `d_data.values()` and `d_data.keys()` in `list()` since Py3 dict views are not directly accepted as positional args to `pd.DataFrame` ## Review & Testing Checklist for Human - [ ] **`push_file` binary read + base64 encoding**: Verify that reading `ASLRay.sh` in `'rb'` mode and calling `base64.b64encode(content).decode('ascii')` produces a valid shell-safe string when interpolated into the `echo ... | base64 -d` command inside the container. This is the only non-trivial Py3 adaptation. - [ ] **`except Exception:` narrowing**: Confirm you're okay with `is_net_on()` no longer catching `KeyboardInterrupt` and `SystemExit`. This is generally better practice but changes behavior if someone Ctrl+C's during the network check. - [ ] **End-to-end test in LXC environment**: These changes could only be syntax-checked and mock-tested ŌĆö no real LXC containers were available. Run `HPC_data_collector.py` (both `aslray_mode = True` and `False`) against a live container to confirm nothing broke. ### Notes - Requested by: @Person543 - [Link to Devin session](https://app.devin.ai/sessions/95e9e385366946d99758392f8a1ee4da) ---

title: feat(launch): migrate ROS 1 XML launch files to ROS 2 Python scripts
body:
## Description
This PR addresses the final Stage (Developer 3) of our ROS 1 to ROS 2 Migration Plan. It converts all legacy [.launch](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/2_world.launch:0:0-0:0) XML files into ROS 2 compliant [.launch.py](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/1_world.launch.py:0:0-0:0) scripts.

## Changes Made
*   **Deleted Legacy Files:** Safely removed outdated ROS 1 `*.launch` files from the [launch/](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/2_world.launch:0:0-0:0) directory ([1_world_and_script.launch](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/1_world_and_script.launch:0:0-0:0), [2_world_and_script.launch](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/2_world_and_script.launch:0:0-0:0), [1_world.launch](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/1_world.launch:0:0-0:0), [2_world.launch](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/2_world.launch:0:0-0:0)).
*   **Created Python Replacements:**
    *   **Gazebo Simulation ([1_world.launch.py](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/1_world.launch.py:0:0-0:0), [2_world.launch.py](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/2_world.launch.py:0:0-0:0))**: Included the `gazebo.launch.py` file from `gazebo_ros` and pointed it to the correct custom `.world` file within our package via the `world` launch argument.
    *   **Full Suite ([1_world_and_script.launch.py](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/1_world_and_script.launch.py:0:0-0:0), [2_world_and_script.launch.py](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/2_world_and_script.launch.py:0:0-0:0))**: Defined an `IncludeLaunchDescription` for Gazebo and instantiated a ROS 2 `Node` for [camera_read.py](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/scripts/camera_read.py:0:0-0:0) (and [decode_qr.py](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/scripts/decode_qr.py:0:0-0:0) respectively) to run in parallel. 
    *   **Environment Variables:** Automatically mapped the `models` directory to the `GAZEBO_MODEL_PATH` explicitly via the `SetEnvironmentVariable` action, ensuring Gazebo correctly discovers locally defined models (like the QR code plate) across machines.

## Dependencies
*   Depends on Stage 1 (ament_cmake refactoring) & Stage 2 (rclpy nodes integration).

body:
Bumps the pip group with 1 update in the /backuppc directory: [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests). Bumps the pip group with 1 update in the /oscam directory: [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests). Bumps the pip group with 1 update in the /tvheadend directory: [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests). Updates `requests` from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • ­¤ōŻ Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. ­¤ōŻ

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

New Contributors

Full Changelog: https://github.com/psf/requests/blob/main/HISTORY.md#2330-2026-03-25

2.32.5 (2025-08-18)

Bugfixes

  • The SSLContext caching feature originally introduced in 2.32.0 has created a new class of issues in Requests that have had negative impact across a number of use cases. The Requests team has decided to revert this feature as long term maintenance of it is proving to be unsustainable in its current iteration.

Deprecations

  • Added support for Python 3.14.
  • Dropped support for Python 3.8 following its end of support.

title: Bump python-dotenv from 1.0.1 to 1.2.2 in /embedding_pipeline
body:
Bumps [python-dotenv](https://github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv) from 1.0.1 to 1.2.2.
Release notes

Sourced from python-dotenv's releases.

v1.2.2

Added

  • Support for Python 3.14, including the free-threaded (3.14t) build. (#)

Changed

  • The dotenv run command now forwards flags directly to the specified command by @ŌĆŗbbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#607
  • Improved documentation clarity regarding override behavior and the reference page.
  • Updated PyPy support to version 3.11.
  • Documentation for FIFO file support.
  • Support for Python 3.9.

Fixed

  • Improved set_key and unset_key behavior when interacting with symlinks by @ŌĆŗbbc2 in #790c5
  • Corrected the license specifier and added missing Python 3.14 classifiers in package metadata by @ŌĆŗJYOuyang in theskumar/python-dotenv#590

Breaking Changes

  • dotenv.set_key and dotenv.unset_key used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case. For that behavior to be restored in all cases, follow_symlinks=True should be used.

  • In the CLI, set and unset used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case.

  • dotenv.set_key, dotenv.unset_key and the CLI commands set and unset used to reset the file mode of the modified .env file to 0o600 in some situations. This is no longer the case: The original mode of the file is now preserved. Is the file needed to be created or wasn't a regular file, mode 0o600 is used.

Misc

  • skip 000 permission tests for root user by @ŌĆŗburnout-projects in theskumar/python-dotenv#561
  • Bump actions/checkout from 5 to 6 in the github-actions group by @ŌĆŗdependabot[bot] in theskumar/python-dotenv#593
  • Add Windows testing to CI by @ŌĆŗbbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#604

v1.2.1

What's Changed

... (truncated)

Changelog

Sourced from python-dotenv's changelog.

[1.2.2] - 2026-03-01

Added

  • Support for Python 3.14, including the free-threaded (3.14t) build. (#588)

Changed

  • The dotenv run command now forwards flags directly to the specified command by [@ŌĆŗbbc2] in #607
  • Improved documentation clarity regarding override behavior and the reference page.
  • Updated PyPy support to version 3.11.
  • Documentation for FIFO file support.
  • Dropped Support for Python 3.9.

Fixed

  • Improved set_key and unset_key behavior when interacting with symlinks by [@ŌĆŗbbc2] in [790c5c0]
  • Corrected the license specifier and added missing Python 3.14 classifiers in package metadata by [@ŌĆŗJYOuyang] in #590

Breaking Changes

  • dotenv.set_key and dotenv.unset_key used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case. For that behavior to be restored in all cases, follow_symlinks=True should be used.

  • In the CLI, set and unset used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case.

  • dotenv.set_key, dotenv.unset_key and the CLI commands set and unset used to reset the file mode of the modified .env file to 0o600 in some situations. This is no longer the case: The original mode of the file is now preserved. Is the file needed to be created or wasn't a regular file, mode 0o600 is used.

[1.2.1] - 2025-10-26

  • Move more config to pyproject.toml, removed setup.cfg
  • Add support for reading .env from FIFOs (Unix) by [@ŌĆŗsidharth-sudhir] in #586

[1.2.0] - 2025-10-26

  • Upgrade build system to use PEP 517 & PEP 518 to use build and pyproject.toml by [@ŌĆŗEpicWink] in #583
  • Add support for Python 3.14 by [@ŌĆŗ23f3001135] in #579
  • Add support for disabling of load_dotenv() using PYTHON_DOTENV_DISABLED env var. by [@ŌĆŗmatthewfranglen] in #569

[1.1.1] - 2025-06-24

Fixed

title: Bump requests from 2.32.3 to 2.33.1 in /infrastructure
body:
Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.32.3 to 2.33.1.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.1

2.33.1 (2026-03-30)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed test cleanup for CVE-2026-25645 to avoid leaving unnecessary files in the tmp directory. (#7305)
  • Fixed Content-Type header parsing for malformed values. (#7309)
  • Improved error consistency for malformed header values. (#7308)

New Contributors

  • @ŌĆŗferdnyc made their first contribution in psf/requests#7277

Full Changelog: https://github.com/psf/requests/blob/main/HISTORY.md#2331-2026-03-30

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • ­¤ōŻ Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. ­¤ōŻ

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

title: PDE-10458: Upgrade dependencies for Python 3.11 compatibility
body:
## Summary
- Bumps `requests`, `singer-python`, `pendulum` to Python 3.11-compatible versions
- Points `tap-kit` at `Radico/tap-kit@main` instead of `dmzobel/tap-kit`
- Removes stale `dependency_links`
- Adds `python_requires=">=3.9"`

## Context
After the Python 3.9ŌåÆ3.11 upgrade (PDE-10351), this tap crashes on import:
```
ImportError: cannot import name 'Mapping' from 'collections'
```
Root cause: `requests==2.18.4` ŌåÆ `urllib3==1.22` uses `collections.Mapping`, removed in Python 3.10.

Jira: [PDE-10458](https://simondata.atlassian.net/browse/PDE-10458)

## Test plan
- [ ] Re-run `pipes-extract-wework-singer-contentful` Jenkins job and confirm it passes
- [ ] Verify extracted data lands in S3 as expected

title: feat: add module-based piscine directory structure
body:
The repository lacked the module-organized structure matching the official 42 Python for Data Science piscine. Exercises existed only as a flat `exercises/` folder with no module separation.

## Structure added

Five module directories, each with zero-padded exercise subfolders (`ex00`ŌĆō`exNN`):

```
python_0_starting/   ex00ŌĆōex09
python_1_array/      ex00ŌĆōex05
python_2_datatable/  ex00ŌĆōex04
python_3_oop/        ex00ŌĆōex04
python_4_dod/        ex00ŌĆōex02
```

## Starter files per exercise

Each exercise folder contains three files following the established repo conventions:

- **`main.py`** ŌĆö module docstring, `main()` entry point, `if __name__ == "__main__":` guard
- **`README.md`** ŌĆö Objective / Instructions / Requirements / Implementation Notes / Example sections
- **`notes.md`** ŌĆö Key Observations / Difficulties / Ideas for Follow-up sections

## Root README

Updated to document the new module layout, explain what the piscine is, describe exercise organization, and update the `python` run path example to match the new structure.

title: Port Telos compiler from Python to C
body:
Full C implementation of the Telos compiler in `csrc/`, preserving all documented semantics: constraint-graph IR, polynomial closed-form detection, constant folding, x86-64 codegen, and C codegen.

### New files (`csrc/`)

- **`telos.h`** ŌĆö All types: tokens, AST, IR, constraint graph, execution plans, public API
- **`lexer.c`** ŌĆö Tokenizer (keywords, two-char operators, comments, float/int literals)
- **`parser.c`** ŌĆö Recursive-descent parser, full expression precedence chain
- **`ir.c`** ŌĆö IR node constructors, deep clone/free, algebraic simplifier, constant evaluator
- **`lifting.c`** ŌĆö AST ŌåÆ constraint IR; accumulation loop ŌåÆ `ReductionConstraint` pattern detection
- **`optimizer.c`** ŌĆö Polynomial decomposition, closed-form summation (degree Ōēż 3), constant folding (Ōēż10k iters), goal-graph liveness / dead-code elimination, cost-model plan selection
- **`codegen_x86.c`** ŌĆö x86-64 machine code emission (System V AMD64 ABI, stack-slot allocation, jump patching)
- **`codegen_c.c`** ŌĆö `long long`-based C source emission
- **`executable.c`** ŌĆö `mmap(PROT_EXEC)` wrapper for native execution
- **`compiler.c`** ŌĆö High-level API (`telos_compile`, `telos_run`, `telos_compile_c`) + CLI
- **`Makefile`** ŌĆö `make` builds `telos`, `make test` runs suite
- **`tests/test_main.c`** ŌĆö 45 tests across lexer, parser, lifting, optimizer, and end-to-end execution

### Verification

All optimizations from the spec are exercised end-to-end:

```bash
$ cd csrc && make test
=== Results: 45 passed, 0 failed, 45 total ===

$ ./telos ../examples/fixed_sum.telos
Function: fixed_sum  (29 bytes of x86-64 machine code)
  # constant-folded to mov rax, 45
```

Covers: Gauss formula (`╬Żi`), sum-of-squares (`╬Żi┬▓`), sum-of-cubes (`╬Żi┬│`), linear combinations, constant folding, inclusive ranges, multi-function files, identity/arithmetic functions.

### Other changes

- **`.gitignore`** ŌĆö Added `csrc/*.o`, `csrc/telos`, `csrc/tests/test_telos`
- **`README.md`** ŌĆö Updated repo layout, added C build/run/test instructions alongside existing Python docs

Python implementation and its 119 tests are untouched and still pass.

title: Add CallGuard AI ŌĆö MeetStream MIA + FastMCP + Scalekit integration
body:
Bootstraps the full CallGuard AI project: a silent call-monitoring agent that scores customer calls for churn risk, refund likelihood, and escalation need, then autonomously creates Jira tickets and sends Gmail alerts via Scalekit-authenticated tool calls.

## Structure

```
callguard-ai/
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ mcp_server/
Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ server.py          # FastMCP server ŌĆö exposes create_jira_ticket + send_gmail at port 8000
Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ Dockerfile         # python:3.11-slim, exposes 8000
Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ requirements.txt   # fastmcp, scalekit-sdk-python, python-dotenv
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ webhook/
Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ main.py            # Flask webhook ŌĆö scores MeetStream transcription events, calls Scalekit tools directly
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ auth_check.py          # One-time interactive OAuth consent flow for Jira + Gmail connected accounts
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ .env.example
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ .gitignore
ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ README.md
```

## Key design points

- **Two execution paths**: MIA agent ŌåÆ MCP server ŌåÆ Scalekit (real-time, LLM-driven) and webhook ŌåÆ Scalekit direct (event-driven fallback for batch/transcript events)
- **Scoring heuristic** in `webhook/main.py` keyword-matches transcript text; thresholds at 1 hit (Medium) and 3 hits (High)
- **Tool routing**: `create_jira_ticket` fires on churn OR escalation High; `send_gmail` only on escalation High, with Jira URL injected into body
- **Auth gating**: `ensure_all_authenticated()` runs at webhook startup ŌĆö blocks boot if Scalekit connected accounts are not active
- **Security**: Flask `debug` mode is off by default; opt-in via `FLASK_DEBUG=true` env var (fixes CodeQL `py/flask-debug`)
- **README** includes full MIA system prompt with explicit tool signatures and scoring rules, plus `create_bot` curl command

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>


## CallGuard AI ŌĆö MeetStream + Scalekit MCP Integration

Create the full CallGuard AI project structure in this repository. This is a complete working integration between MeetStream MIA (AI meeting agent), a Docker-based FastMCP server, and Scalekit for authenticated tool calling (Jira + Gmail).

### Architecture

```
MeetStream MIA Agent
        Ōåō  (MCP Server URL = ngrok URL pointing to Docker)
Docker MCP Server ŌĆö FastMCP (port 8000)
        Ōåō  (tool calls: create_jira_ticket, send_gmail)
Scalekit ŌĆö validates OAuth tokens, executes tools on behalf of user
        Ōåō
Jira + Gmail
```

### File Structure to Create

```
callguard-ai/
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ mcp_server/
Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ server.py
Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ Dockerfile
Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ requirements.txt
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ webhook/
Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ main.py
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ auth_check.py
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ .env.example
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ .gitignore
ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ README.md
```

---

### `mcp_server/server.py`

```python
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from fastmcp import FastMCP
from scalekit.client import ScalekitClient

load_dotenv(dotenv_path="../.env")

IDENTIFIER       = os.getenv("IDENTIFIER", "user-001")
JIRA_CONNECTION  = os.getenv("JIRA_CONNECTION_NAME", "jira")
GMAIL_CONNECTION = os.getenv("GMAIL_CONNECTION_NAME", "gmail")

scalekit = ScalekitClient(
    env_url=os.getenv("SCALEKIT_ENV_URL"),
    client_id=os.getenv("SCALEKIT_CLIENT_ID"),
    client_secret=os.getenv("SCALEKIT_CLIENT_SECRET"),
)

mcp = FastMCP("callguard-tools")


@mcp.tool()
def create_jira_ticket(summary: str, description: str, priority: str = "High") -> str:
    """
    Create a Jira ticket for a high-risk or escalation call.
    Use when churn risk is High or escalation need is High.
    Parameters:
    - summary: One-line title e.g. "[High Escalation] Customer threatening to cancel ŌĆö 2026-03-28"
    - description: Full breakdown including churn risk, refund likelihood, escalation need, and a 2-3 sentence call summary
    - priority: "High", "Medium", or "Low" ŌĆö match to the highest score across all three dimensions
    """
    result = scalekit.actions.execute_tool(
        tool_name="jira_issue_create",
        identifier=IDENTIFIER,
        tool_input={
            "project_key": os.getenv("JIRA_PROJECT_KEY", "CG"),
            "summary": summary,
            "description": description,
            "issue_type": "Task",
            "priority": priority,
        },
    )
    return f"Jira ticket created: {result.data.get('url', 'done')}"


@mcp.tool()
def send_gmail(to: str, subject: str, body: str) -> str:
    """
    Send a Gmail alert to a stakeholder about a critical call outcome.
    Use when escalation need is High. Include Jira ticket URL in body if one was created.
    Parameters:
    - to: The escalation email address
    - subject: e.g. "­¤Ü© CallGuard Alert ŌĆö High Escalation on call 2026-03-28"
    - body: Include churn risk, refund likelihood, escalation need, call summary, and Jira ticket URL
    """
    scalekit.actions.execute_tool(
        tool_name="gmail_send_email",
        identifier=IDENTIFIER,
        tool_input={"to": to, "subject": subject, "body": body},
    )
    return f"Email sent to {to}"


if __name__ == "__main__":
    mcp.run(transport="streamable-http", host="0.0.0.0", port=8000)
```

---

### `mcp_server/requirements.txt`

```
fastmcp>=2.0.0
scalekit-sdk-python>=2.6.1
python-dotenv>=1.1.1
```

---

### `mcp_server/Dockerfile`

```dockerfile
FROM python:3.11-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
COPY server.py .
EXPOSE 8000
CMD ["python", "server.py"]
```

---

### `webhook/main.py`

```python
import os
import json
import requests
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from flask import Flask, request
from scalekit.client import ScalekitClient

MEETSTREAM_BASE_URL = "https://api.meetstream.ai/api/v1"

    try:

    except Exception as e:
        raise RuntimeError(

        ) from e

    account = resp.connected_account
    if account.status != "ACTIVE":
        link = scalekit.actions.get_authorization_link(

## Summary by cubic
Create the full CallGuard project structure. Adds a Dockerized `fastmcp` MCP server wired to Jira/Gmail via `scalekit` and a Flask webhook for MeetStream events, plus setup and auth utilities.

- **New Features**
  - `mcp_server/` with `server.py` exposing `create_jira_ticket` and `send_gmail` via `scalekit`; runs on port 8000 over streamable HTTP.
  - `mcp_server/Dockerfile` and `requirements.txt` (`fastmcp`, `scalekit-sdk-python`, `python-dotenv`).
  - `webhook/` Flask service (port 8999) to receive MeetStream events, verify Scalekit connections, score transcripts, and trigger tools.
  - `.env.example`, `.gitignore`, `README.md` (full setup), and `auth_check.py` to authorize Jira + Gmail; Flask debug is off by default (`FLASK_DEBUG`).

- **Migration**
  - Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and set `SCALEKIT_*`, `IDENTIFIER`, `JIRA_PROJECT_KEY`, `MEET_STREAM_API_KEY`, `ESCALATION_EMAIL` (optional `FLASK_DEBUG`).
  - Authorize Jira and Gmail with `python auth_check.py` or when prompted by the webhook.
  - Build/run MCP server: `docker build -t callguard-mcp ./mcp_server && docker run --env-file ./.env -p 8000:8000 callguard-mcp`.
  - Expose MCP on ngrok and set that URL as the MCP Server URL in MeetStream; start the webhook on 8999, expose it, and register the webhook URL in MeetStream.

title: Adjust dependencies for Python 3.14 compatibility
body:
### Motivation
- Avoid build failures of `pydantic-core` on Python 3.14 by using FastAPI and Pydantic versions that do not require compiling `pydantic-core`.

### Description
- Update `requirements.txt` to pin `fastapi==0.103.2` and `pydantic==1.10.18` while keeping `uvicorn==0.30.6` and `requests==2.32.3`.

### Testing
- Ran `pip install -r requirements.txt` which completed successfully and then verified the FastAPI app import with `python - <<'PY'
from app.api.main import app
print(app.title)
PY` which printed the expected application title.

------

title: Fix pydub on Python 3.13+ and eliminate per-entry ffmpeg errors
body:
Two related issues: `audioop` was removed in Python 3.13 (PEP 594), breaking pydub's import entirely; and when ffmpeg is missing from PATH, the script emits `ERROR: pydub processing failed: [WinError 2]` once per Pok├®mon entry (~532├Ś) instead of failing fast.

## `tools/requirements.txt`
- Add `audioop-lts; python_version>="3.13"` ŌĆö LTS backport of `audioop`, no-op on older Pythons

## `tools/generate_voices.py`
- Add `HAS_FFMPEG = HAS_PYDUB and bool(shutil.which("ffmpeg") or shutil.which("avconv"))` checked once at module load
- Gate pydub processing on `HAS_PYDUB and HAS_FFMPEG`; raw-audio fallback runs silently (startup warning is sufficient)
- Suppress pydub's own `RuntimeWarning: Couldn't find ffmpeg` at import time via `warnings.catch_warnings()` ŌĆö replaced by our single, OS-specific startup warning
- Startup warning block gains an `elif not HAS_FFMPEG` branch with actionable install instructions per OS

## `README.md`
- Requirements table: note Python 3.13+ support via `audioop-lts`; fix install path to `tools/requirements.txt`
- Troubleshooting table: replace vague `ffmpeg not found` row with specific rows for `[WinError 2]`, `ffmpeg not found on PATH`, and the Python 3.13 `audioop` ImportError

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

## Problem

On Python 3.13+, the built-in `audioop` module was removed (PEP 594). This causes `pydub` to fail to import because it depends on `audioop` internally. The error manifests as:

```
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'audioop'
```

followed by:

```
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pyaudioop'
```

Because the script catches this `ImportError` and sets `HAS_PYDUB = False`, users see a misleading warning: `"pydub is not installed"` even though pydub IS installed ŌĆö it just can't load without `audioop`.

## Fix

Add `audioop-lts` as a conditional dependency in `tools/requirements.txt`. This is the community-maintained LTS port of the removed `audioop` module for Python 3.13+.

Add the following line to `tools/requirements.txt` under the audio processing section (near the `pydub` dependency):

```
audioop-lts; python_version>="3.13"
```

This ensures the dependency is only installed on Python 3.13+ where `audioop` is missing, and has no effect on older Python versions.

Also update the README.md if it contains any relevant installation or troubleshooting instructions to mention this Python 3.13+ requirement.

The following is the prior conversation context from the user's chat exploration (may be truncated):

User: It keeps saying pydub not installed but it is

Assistant: Now I can see the issue. Looking at the code in `generate_voices.py`, here's what's happening:

## The Problem

The script detects `pydub` at **import time** (lines 82ŌĆō87) by setting a global `HAS_PYDUB` flag:

```python name=tools/generate_voices.py url=https://github.com/SilvertongueRED/KIF-Pokedex-Voice-Over/blob/2dc4bf32850c3ffdf09edb32675b3faa695d1597/tools/generate_voices.py#L82-L87
try:
    from pydub import AudioSegment
    from pydub.effects import normalize
    HAS_PYDUB = True
except ImportError:
    HAS_PYDUB = False
```

Even though `pydub` the Python package is installed, the import can **silently fail** if **ffmpeg is not installed or not on your PATH**. When `pydub` loads, it tries to locate `ffmpeg`/`ffprobe`, and some versions or configurations raise an `ImportError` or a downstream error during the import of submodules when ffmpeg is missing.

This causes `HAS_PYDUB` to be `False`, and when the script runs it prints (line 633ŌĆō638):

> `WARNING: pydub is not installed ŌĆö Dexter audio effects will be skipped.`

## The Fix

**Install ffmpeg and make sure it's on your system PATH:**

| OS | Command |
|---|---|
| **Windows** | Download from [ffmpeg.org](https://ffmpeg.org/download.html), extract, and add the `bin/` folder to your system PATH. Or use `choco install ffmpeg` / `winget install ffmpeg`. |
| **macOS** | `brew install ffmpeg` |
| **Linux (Debian/Ubuntu)** | `sudo apt install ffmpeg` |

After installing ffmpeg, verify it works:
```bash
ffmpeg -version
```

Then re-run the script ŌĆö `pydub` should import successfully and `HAS_PYDUB` will be `True`.

## How to confirm this is the issue

You can test it quickly in Python:

```bash
python -c "from pydub import AudioSegment; from pydub.effects import normalize; print('pydub OK')"
```

If this prints `pydub OK`, the script should work. If it throws an error mentioning ffmpeg/ffprobe, that confirms ffmpeg is the missing piece.

ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'audioop'

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):

    from pydub import AudioSegment; from pydub.effects import normalize; print('pydub OK')
    ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "C:\Users\rolan\AppData\Roaming\Python\Python314\site-packages\pydub\__init__.py", line 1, in <module>

  File "C:\Users\rolan\AppData\Roaming\Python\Python314\site-packages\pydub\audio_segment.py", line 11, in <module>
    from .utils import mediainfo_json, fsdecode
  File "C:\Users\rolan\AppData\Roaming\Python\Python314\site-packages\pydub\utils.py", line 16, in <module>

ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pyaudioop'
PS C:\Users\rolan>

Assistant: ## Root Cause

This is a **Python version incompatibility**, not a pydub installation problem.

You're running **Python 3.14**, which (along with 3.13) **removed the built-in `audioop` module** that pydub depends on ([PEP 594](https://peps.python.org/pep-0594/)). You can see pydub trying two things and both failing:

1. `import audioop` ŌåÆ ŌØī removed from Python 3.13+
2. `import pyaudioop as audioop` ŌåÆ ŌØī not installed

Because this import fails, the entire `from pydub import AudioSegment` raises `ImportError`, which the script's `try/except` catche...

</details>

title: fix: upgrade pydantic and openai for Python 3.13 compatibility
body:
Railway deploys on Python 3.13, which is incompatible with pydantic-core==2.16.3 (bundled with pydantic==2.6.4). The build failed because pydantic-core had no pre-built wheel for Python 3.13, forcing a Rust/maturin source compilation that crashed due to a breaking change in ForwardRef._evaluate() in Python 3.13.

Bumped versions to packages that ship pre-built py3.13 wheels:
- pydantic: 2.6.4 ŌåÆ 2.7.4
- openai:   1.14.0 ŌåÆ 1.30.0
- fastapi:  0.110.0 ŌåÆ 0.115.0

No source code changes required ŌĆö purely a dependency version fix.

title: Fix Railway build failure and security vulnerabilities in requirements.txt
body:
Railway's default Python 3.13 runtime rejected the pinned dependencies ŌĆö `numpy`, `matplotlib`, `kiwisolver`, and `contourpy` had no Python 3.13 wheels and failed to compile from source.

## Dependency upgrades

| Package | Old | New | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|
| `numpy` | 1.24.3 | 2.2.4 | 1.24.x tops out at Python 3.11; 2.x required for 3.13 |
| `matplotlib` | 3.7.1 | 3.9.4 | Python 3.13 wheels available from 3.8+ |
| `kiwisolver` | 1.4.4 | 1.4.8 | Python 3.13 wheels |
| `contourpy` | 1.0.7 | 1.3.1 | Python 3.13 wheels |
| `Pillow` | 9.5.0 | 12.1.1 | Python 3.13 wheels + CVE: out-of-bounds write in PSD loader |
| `fonttools` | 4.39.4 | 4.55.3 | Python 3.13 wheels |
| `Django` | 4.2.1 | 4.2.29 | CVE: SQL injection in column aliases and `_connector` kwarg; DoS in `HttpResponseRedirect` on Windows |
| `asgiref`, `cycler`, `packaging`, `pyparsing`, `sqlparse`, `tzdata` | various | bumped | Routine updates alongside above |

## New files

- **`.python-version`** ŌĆö pins `3.13` so Railway/Nixpacks uses an explicit version rather than defaulting to whatever is latest at build time.

title: ci(release): fix build step running outside semantic-release container
body:
## Summary

- Remove `build_command` from `[tool.semantic_release]` ŌĆö the Docker container doesn't have `build` installed
- Add dedicated `setup-python` + `pip install build && python -m build` step in the workflow
- semantic-release only does version bump + tag + commit
- Build and publish happen in separate steps on the GitHub Actions runner

## Test plan

- [ ] Release workflow completes without `No module named build` error

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Move Python package build to the GitHub Actions runner to fix release failures (ŌĆ£No module named buildŌĆØ) caused by running inside the `semantic-release` container. `semantic-release` now only handles versioning; build and publish run as separate steps.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Removed `build_command` and publish settings from `[tool.semantic_release]` in `pyproject.toml`.
  - Added `actions/setup-python@v5` and `pip install build && python -m build` steps, gated on a release.
  - Kept publishing via `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1` on the runner.

<sup>Written for commit 002cdacdc84c8d238aa5041cac89f2fcbbf48adc. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

title: Migrate to GitHub Actions CI
body:
## Migrate CI from Travis CI to GitHub Actions

Travis CI was inherited from the upstream orator project and tested Python 2.7ŌĆō3.7 ŌĆö versions incompatible with this fork's python = "^3.10" requirement. Travis is also effectively unavailable for open-source projects. This PR replaces it with GitHub Actions.

Changes:

- Add .github/workflows/tests.yml with two jobs:
- unit-tests ŌĆö runs across Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13, 3.14 (no external services needed)
- integration-tests ŌĆö runs across Python 3.10 and 3.12 using Docker Compose to spin up Postgres 14 and MySQL 8
- Delete .travis.yml
- Delete tox.ini (also stale, same Python 2.7ŌĆō3.7 matrix)
- Fix exit-code masking in make test and make test-int ŌĆö previously docker-down ran via ; which swallowed a failing pytest exit code; now the exit code is captured and re-raised after cleanup

title: [codex] migrate Python tooling to uv
body:
## Summary
This change migrates the repository from the old single `requirements.txt` workflow to a root `uv` project backed by `pyproject.toml` and `uv.lock`, and adds a repo-level `Makefile` to make the common development commands easier to use.

For contributors, the practical effect is that Python setup, test execution, app startup, and script usage now run through `uv` consistently from the repository root. The repo still behaves as one shared Python application spanning the existing backend modules, so the migration improves dependency management and local reproducibility without forcing a risky package split.

## Problem
The repository was still carrying a mixed Python toolchain story: a single `requirements.txt`, shell-default interpreter drift, and docs/scripts that assumed `pip`-style installation or direct `python scripts/...` execution. That made local setup less reproducible and made it too easy to end up on the wrong interpreter or with imports that only worked incidentally from a specific shell context.

At the same time, the project needed a lighter-weight contributor workflow. The new `Makefile` provides short aliases for the commands people are actually using repeatedly, while keeping the underlying `uv` commands explicit and visible.

## Root Cause
The Python side of the monorepo had never been formalized as a root project. It relied on repo-root execution conventions and implicit import behavior instead of an explicit dependency manager and lockfile.

That was especially visible in direct script execution and pytest collection: once the repo moved under `uv`, commands needed a stable way to resolve the repo-root modules (`api`, `agents`, `shared`) from the root project environment.

## Fix
The change introduces a root `pyproject.toml` with Python 3.12 constraints, first-party runtime dependencies, and a dev dependency group, then generates and commits `uv.lock`. The old `requirements.txt` file is removed.

The docs and script/help text were updated to standardize on `uv sync`, `uv run pytest tests`, `uv run python -m uvicorn ...`, and `uv run python scripts/...` commands. A concise `AGENTS.md` was also rewritten to reflect the repo's real toolchain and constraints.

To keep the current monorepo shape working safely, the Python project remains a single shared app rather than being split into multiple Python packages. Tests and direct repo scripts were updated so imports continue to resolve correctly from the root `uv` environment.

The new `Makefile` adds short, repo-friendly commands for setup, tests, local servers, smoke checks, formatting, linting, type checking, and registry sync. `make check` is positioned as the practical passing verification set, while `make lint` and `make typecheck` are available to expose the existing repo-wide backlog.

## Validation
I validated the migration with the commands below:

- `uv sync`
- `uv run pytest tests -q`
- `uv run python scripts/sync_agents_from_registry.py --help`
- `uv run python -m uvicorn api.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8010`
- `uv run python -m uvicorn agents.research.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5002`
- `make help`
- `make check`

## Notes
`make lint` / `ruff` and `make typecheck` / `mypy` are intentionally exposed in the `Makefile`, but they still report pre-existing repo-wide issues and were not treated as merge-blocking for this migration.

title: Bump requests from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0
body:
Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • ­¤ōŻ Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. ­¤ōŻ

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

title: Apply manual Python3 fixes (2to3-equivalent)
Fix CI: Python 3.10 compatibility and black formatting drift
body:
Two CI failures on the `copilot/fix-module-not-found-error` branch: `datetime.UTC` (Python 3.11+) caused `ImportError` on the Python 3.10 runners, and 9 files had accumulated Black formatting drift.

## Changes

- **`datetime.UTC` ŌåÆ `timezone.utc`** across all affected files ŌĆö `datetime.UTC` was introduced in Python 3.11; replaced with `timezone.utc` (available since Python 3.2) to restore 3.10 compatibility:
  ```python
  # Before (Python 3.11+ only)
  from datetime import UTC, datetime
  return datetime.now(UTC)

  # After
  from datetime import datetime, timezone
  return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
  ```
  Files affected: `auth/model.py`, `finance/models.py`, `finance/routes.py`, `finance/service.py`, `tests/test_finance.py`

- **Black formatting** ŌĆö ran `black .` to bring all 9 drifted files into conformance with the enforced style check

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

­¤Æ¼ Send tasks to Copilot coding agent from [Slack](https://gh.io/cca-slack-docs) and [Teams](https://gh.io/cca-teams-docs) to turn conversations into code. Copilot posts an update in your thread when it's finished.

title: Switch to self-hosted TrueNAS Scale architecture
body:
This PR shifts the project from a Vercel-hosted deployment model to a fully self-hosted Docker Compose setup designed for TrueNAS Scale.

### Changes Included:
1. **Architecture shift:** The Vue 3 SPA is now statically compiled during a multi-stage Docker build and served by the FastAPI backend on a single port (`8000`). This eliminates the need for separate frontend hosting like Vercel or Render.
2. **Backend Proxying & Database:** External calls to Overpass and OSRM have been migrated from the frontend to the Python backend to avoid client rate-limiting and allow storing results locally in a PostGIS database.
3. **Docker Compose Setup:** A complete `docker-compose.yml` is provided alongside a `.env.example` file. It orchestrates the API server, a PostGIS database, Redis, and Valhalla routing.
4. **Database Configuration:** Uses `postgresql+asyncpg` for asynchronous database connections. Lifecycle events handle creating database metadata on startup.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [15512511679198520980](https://jules.google.com/task/15512511679198520980) started by @USBKayble*

title: Fix Python 2.7 virtualenv PermissionError and Passenger crash-loop lock contention
body:
Two cPanel deployment failures: (1) virtualenv creation fails with `PermissionError` on CloudLinux Python 2.7 `.pyc` files; (2) any startup exception puts Passenger into a crash-restart loop, blocking cPanel's app management lock and causing `Can't acquire lock for app: FakeNTP-cpanel` on operations like **Run Script**.

## Python version enforcement

- `passenger_wsgi.py` / `cli.py` ŌĆö fail-fast guard before any imports:
  ```python
  if sys.version_info < (3, 8):
      raise RuntimeError("FakeNTP requires Python 3.8 or later. ...")
  ```
- `pyproject.toml` ŌĆö added `requires-python = ">=3.8"` under a `[project]` section

## Passenger crash-loop prevention

Wraps `create_app()` in `passenger_wsgi.py` with a `try/except` that keeps Passenger alive on any startup failure by serving a `500` fallback instead of crashing:

```python
try:
    from fakentp.app import create_app
    application = create_app()
except Exception as _exc:
    _log.critical("FakeNTP failed to initialize: %s", _exc, exc_info=True)
    _msg = ("FakeNTP failed to start: {}\n"
            "Check the server error log for details.".format(_exc)).encode()

    def application(environ, start_response):
        start_response("500 Internal Server Error", [...])
        return [_msg]
```

This stops the restart loop so cPanel can acquire the management lock for **Run Script** / package installs even when the app is misconfigured.

## Documentation

Added a **Troubleshooting** section to `README.md` covering both errors with cause and fix steps.

title: Add README_SETUP.md: Windows public deployment + iOS Shortcuts integration guide
body:
Adds `README_SETUP.md` ŌĆö a comprehensive setup guide derived directly from the actual source files (`.env.example`, `config.py`, `scripts/gmail_auth.py`, endpoint schemas).

## Part 1 ŌĆö Windows Setup for Public Usage
- **Prerequisites** ŌĆö Python 3.12+, catch-all domain concept, Google Cloud project
- **Env config** ŌĆö full table of every `.env` variable with must-change flags; secure token generation via `secrets.token_urlsafe(32)`
- **Gmail OAuth** ŌĆö complete Cloud Console walkthrough: project ŌåÆ Gmail API ŌåÆ Desktop OAuth creds ŌåÆ `credentials.json` ŌåÆ `python scripts/gmail_auth.py` ŌåÆ `token.json`
- **Catch-all domain** ŌĆö Cloudflare Email Routing, Namecheap, and generic providers
- **Public exposure** ŌĆö Cloudflare Tunnel (recommended), ngrok, nginx+Certbot on VPS, PaaS (Railway/Render/Fly.io)
- **Windows service** ŌĆö NSSM install/configure/start so the server survives reboots
- **PostgreSQL upgrade** ŌĆö install, create DB/user, swap `DATABASE_URL`, `alembic upgrade head`
- **Security checklist** ŌĆö 9 items (ADMIN_TOKEN rotation, HTTPS enforcement, firewall, token.json hygiene, etc.)

## Part 2 ŌĆö iOS Shortcuts Integration
- 8 exact Shortcut actions: POST session ŌåÆ extract `session_id`/`client_token`/`alias` ŌåÆ show alias ŌåÆ 30-iteration polling loop with `extracted`/`expired`/`cancelled` branching ŌåÆ 5 s wait ŌåÆ timeout alert
- Variable reference table, cancel-button pattern (calls `POST .../cancel`), and troubleshooting matrix

## Part 3 ŌĆö End-to-End Verification
- 7-step PowerShell curl walkthrough from server start through code extraction confirming all components work together

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->

Use ALL of the following actual source files from the repo to ensure accuracy:

### `scripts/gmail_auth.py` ŌĆö the OAuth setup script

### `Dockerfile`

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
