title: feat: migrate DoltgreSQL to Dolt MySQL
body:
## Summary

Migrate acme-health from DoltgreSQL (PostgreSQL wire protocol, beta) to Dolt MySQL (MySQL wire protocol, mature). Smoke testing the original DoltgreSQL setup hit three blockers: no SQL isolation levels, missing `EXCLUDED` keyword for UPSERTs, and underdocumented environment variables. Dolt's MySQL wire has been stable since 2019 and has native .NET Aspire integration via `AddMySql`.

17 files changed across 5 commits. All tests pass (1 C# unit + 29 Python).

## Changes

**Phase 1 ŌĆö AppHost Infrastructure** (`db58af0`)
- `AddContainer("doltgresql")` ŌåÆ `AddMySql("dolt").WithImage("dolthub/dolt-sql-server", "v1.50.2")`
- `AddParameter("dolt-password", secret: true)` with User Secrets for credential management
- `AddDatabase("acme-health")` for auto DB creation
- Manual `ReferenceExpression` replaced with native `WithReference(db)` on all 4 services
- `DOLT_ROOT_HOST=%` for Docker network access (Dolt v1.46+ security)
- Python endpoint fix: `WithEndpoint` replaces `WithHttpEndpoint` (aspire#14408)

**Phase 2 ŌĆö FhirIngest MySQL Migration** (`b968c22`)
- Npgsql 9.0.3 ŌåÆ MySqlConnector 2.4.0
- `$N` positional params ŌåÆ `@name` named params
- Row alias UPSERT syntax (`AS new ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE col = new.col`)
- Schema DDL: `TEXT PRIMARY KEY` ŌåÆ `VARCHAR(255)`, inline FK ŌåÆ `CONSTRAINT FOREIGN KEY`
- `DOLT_COMMIT` param: `$1` ŌåÆ `@msg`

**Phase 3 ŌĆö Wearable Normalizer MySQL Migration** (`adf0a9b`)
- psycopg ŌåÆ aiomysql
- `parse_connection_string()` returns `dict` (aiomysql kwargs) instead of DSN string
- aiomysql connection pattern: explicit `connect(**kwargs)` / `commit()` / `close()`
- DDL: `SERIAL` ŌåÆ `INT AUTO_INCREMENT`
- clinical-extractor dependency swapped (stub service, no logic changes)

**Phase 4 ŌĆö Documentation** (`12eaec4`)
- ADR-1001 superseded with migration rationale (3 blocking DoltgreSQL gaps)
- README architecture diagram, technology table, driver names updated
- Sequence diagrams: participant labels changed

**CR Polish** (`9261b07`)
- Removed hardcoded password from appsettings.json ŌåÆ User Secrets
- Replaced deprecated `VALUES()` with row alias syntax for MySQL 8.0.20+ forward compat
- Restructured `create_schema()` for cleaner connection cleanup
- Added user-secrets setup step to README

## Quality Evidence

| Phase | Review Score | Challenger | Tests |
|-------|-------------|------------|-------|
| 1: AppHost | 9.3/10 | 5 approved, 0 challenges | `dotnet build` clean |
| 2: FhirIngest | 9.1/10 | 10 approved, 0 challenges | 1 unit pass |
| 3: Wearable | 9.3/10 | 12 approved, 0 challenges | 29/29 pytest pass |
| 4: Docs | 9.5/10 | ŌĆö | grep validation |
| CR Polish | ŌĆö | ŌĆö | all tests re-verified |

**CodeRabbit CLI**: 4 findings identified and resolved before PR creation.

## Testing

- [x] `dotnet build AcmeHealth.slnx` ŌĆö 0 errors, 0 warnings
- [x] `dotnet test --filter "Category!=Integration"` ŌĆö 1 passed
- [x] `uv run pytest` (wearable-normalizer) ŌĆö 29 passed
- [ ] End-to-end with Docker ŌĆö deferred to smoke test (acme-svu.1)

## Review Guidance

| Priority | Files | Why |
|----------|-------|-----|
| High | `src/AppHost/AppHost.cs` | Aspire orchestration ŌĆö all service wiring |
| High | `src/Acme.Stack.FhirIngest/Program.cs` | Largest code change ŌĆö SQL dialect, params |
| Medium | `src/services/wearable-normalizer/main.py` | Python driver swap, connection parser |
| Medium | `src/Acme.Stack.Core/Models.cs` | Shared schema DDL |
| Low | `tests/`, `docs/`, `README.md` | Mechanical updates |

## Security

| Concern | Mitigation |
|---------|------------|
| Database credentials | `AddParameter(secret: true)` + `dotnet user-secrets` ŌĆö no secrets in source |
| Docker network | `DOLT_ROOT_HOST=%` scoped to container network only |

## Spec

Driven by [dolt-mysql-migration](https://github.com/peterkc/acme-sdk/tree/specs/dolt-mysql-migration) ŌĆö 5 phases, consensus score 9.0/10 from triple-model evaluation.


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
  * Migrated database platform from PostgreSQL/DoltgreSQL to MySQL/Dolt with schema and type adjustments for MySQL compatibility.
  * Replaced DB drivers and updated connection handling, parameterization, and commit/versioning to MySQL semantics.
  * Updated container/service orchestration to use a MySQL-based Dolt instance with configurable password and database reference wiring.
  * Aligned runtime configs, tests, and dependency manifests to MySQL/aiomysql connection formats and behaviors.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

title: Create faithful Python port of C inference engine, reorganize versions, update README
body:
The Python version (`postgpt_q.py`) diverged significantly from the canonical C implementation ŌĆö different Dario coefficients, missing best-of-3 generation, no Schumann resonance, no memory persistence, dynamic chamber modulation instead of static, DOE alpha 2├Ś stronger. Created a faithful 1:1 Python port and preserved the research version separately.

### Changes

- **`postgpt_q.py` ŌåÆ `qresearch.py`** ŌĆö old research/training version (Val autograd, RoPE, wormholes, interference, dynamic Dario modulation) preserved under new name
- **New `postgpt_q.py`** (1449 lines) ŌĆö faithful port of `postgpt_q.c`:
  - Same constants (`DOE_ALPHA=0.05`, `TOP_K=15`, `QPTQ_MAGIC=0x51505451`)
  - Same static Dario coefficients (`heb=0.4/0.8`, `bg=5.0/15.0` with/without weights)
  - Same pipeline: best-of-3 coherence scoring, greedyŌåÆnucleus hybrid, age-based repetition penalty, bigram blocking, Schumann resonance, `q.memory` persistence
  - No training, no autograd, no RoPE ŌĆö pure inference matching C
- **`assets/`** ŌĆö browser screenshots of `q.html` (start, generation, field view)
- **`README.md`** rewritten:
  - Author credit, versions table with canonical status
  - Feature comparison table (canonical vs research)
  - Inline browser screenshots
  - `.gitignore` updated for `__pycache__`

### Key differences resolved (C canonical ŌåÆ Python port)

| Feature | Old `postgpt_q.py` | New (matches C) |
|---------|-------------------|-----------------|
| Dario bigram coeff | 12.0 fixed | 5.0/15.0 (tf/no-tf) |
| DOE alpha | 0.1 | 0.05 |
| Best-of-3 | ŌØī | Ō£ģ |
| Schumann resonance | ŌØī | Ō£ģ |
| Memory persistence | ŌØī | Ō£ģ q.memory |
| Rep penalty | uniform 0.5 | age-based 0.3+0.035├Śage |

### Browser version screenshots

title: Break apart install script
body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
## Summary by cubic
Split the Bash installer into a thin bootstrap and a Python-based installer for clearer flow, better UX, and testability. Users keep the same flags and get a safer CLI install, improved prompts, Docker Compose v1/v2 support, and reliable port 25 reachability checks with cloud guidance.

- **Refactors**
  - `install.sh` now handles Docker, firewall, repo clone, Python check, then execs `python3 -m installer.main` (forwards all flags; only `--dir` parsed in Bash; install dir passed via `PRIMITIVEMAIL_DIR`).
  - Moved logic into `installer/config.py`, `installer/server.py`, and `installer/ui.py` (TTY-safe prompts via `/dev/tty`, spinner with tail-on-error, build/start, port 25 checks with cloud hints and local fallback, and `docker compose`/`docker-compose` detection).
  - Pure functions for `.env` generation, summaries, DNS/next steps, and spoof-protection validation; `.env` is written with 0600 perms.

- **New Features**
  - Auto-install Python 3 on `apt`/`yum`/`dnf` systems; install URL updated to `https://get.primitive.dev`.
  - CLI installs to `~/.local/bin`, ensures PATH in `.bashrc`/`.profile`/`.zshrc`, and best-effort symlink to `/usr/local/bin/primitive` (no-prompt sudo if available).
  - Added tests in `test_installer.py` covering env/config generation, IP detection, spoof protection, and DNS/next-steps summaries.

<sup>Written for commit d66c1c7ffef965f8e492497cf0918a8cd8034c89. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: Add /healthz endpoint and stabilize Render start/health check
body:
### Motivation
- Prevent deploy-healthcheck failures and environment drift by providing a lightweight health endpoint and ensuring the app binds the platform-assigned port reliably.

### Description
- Add a `GET /healthz` endpoint in `main.py` that returns `{"status": "ok"}` for fast health probes.
- Update `render.yaml` to run Uvicorn using the platform port (`$PORT`) and change `healthCheckPath` to `/healthz`.
- Pin Render Python runtime by adding `PYTHON_VERSION: 3.11.11` to `render.yaml` to reduce runtime drift.

### Testing
- Ran `python -m py_compile main.py` which succeeded.
- Launched the app locally with Uvicorn and verified `GET /healthz` returned `{"status":"ok"}` using `curl`, which succeeded.
- Confirmed the server responds on the configured port when started with the updated `startCommand`.

------

Announcements

  • ­¤ōŻ Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. ­¤ōŻ

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

title: Add auto-discovery system: eliminate hardcoded backend IP in ESP32 firmware
body:
ESP32 firmware had a hardcoded backend IP (`10.83.60.246`) requiring a reflash on every laptop IP change. This adds a 4-layer runtime discovery system so the device resolves the backend automatically on boot.

## Discovery flow (ESP32, ordered by priority)

1. **mDNS** ŌĆö resolve `chetan-robot.local` (3 retries)
2. **UDP broadcast** ŌĆö probe `255.255.255.255:54321`, parse `CHETAN_ROBOT_BACKEND:<ip>:<port>` reply (2 retries)
3. **NVS/EEPROM** ŌĆö try last-saved IP (persisted by whichever method succeeded previously)
4. **AP mode fallback** ŌĆö creates `ChetanRobot_SETUP` hotspot + captive portal at `192.168.4.1`; user submits backend IP via browser; reboots into normal flow

Each resolved address is verified against `/health` before being accepted and saved to NVS.

## Backend changes
- `app/discovery/mdns_advertiser.py` ŌĆö registers service as `chetan-robot._http._tcp.local.` via `zeroconf`
- `app/discovery/udp_broadcaster.py` ŌĆö async UDP listener on port `54321` responding to discovery probes
- `app/main.py` lifespan ŌĆö starts/stops both services alongside existing monitors
- `requirements.txt` ŌĆö adds `zeroconf==0.131.0`

## ESP32 firmware changes (`espcod/ESPCAM/`)
- `discovery.h` ŌĆö orchestrator; calls all four layers in sequence
- `mdns_handler.h` ŌĆö ESPmDNS service query + hostname fallback
- `udp_discovery.h` ŌĆö broadcast probe + response parser
- `eeprom_config.h` ŌĆö NVS read/write via `Preferences`
- `ap_mode.h` ŌĆö softAP + DNS captive redirect + `WebServer` config form
- `ESPCAM.ino` ŌĆö removes hardcoded IP/device-ID; calls `discover_backend()` post-WiFi; device ID now derived from MAC (`espcam_XXYYZZ`); fixes `is_last` bitwise-OR bug (`|` ŌåÆ `.as<bool>()`)

```cpp
// Before
const char BACKEND_HOST[] = "10.83.60.246";
const char DEVICE_ID[]    = "camcontroller";

// After ŌĆö resolved at runtime
char     BACKEND_HOST[64] = "";
uint16_t BACKEND_PORT     = 8000;
char     DEVICE_ID[32]    = "espcam";  // populated from MAC in setup()

// In setup(), after WiFi connects:
discover_backend(BACKEND_HOST, sizeof(BACKEND_HOST), &BACKEND_PORT);
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

# Auto-Discovery WiFi & mDNS System for ESP32-Backend Connection

## Problem
Currently, the system requires:
1. Hardcoded backend IP address in ESPCAM.ino (line 11: `const char BACKEND_HOST[] = "10.83.60.246"`)
2. Manual IP update every time laptop IP changes
3. Dependency on external hotspot/WiFi network
4. No device discovery mechanism

## Solution Requirements
Implement a **multi-layered auto-discovery system** without external dependencies:

### Option 1: mDNS (Multicast DNS) - Primary Solution
- Backend advertises as `chetan-robot.local` over mDNS
- ESP32 discovers backend using `chetan_robot.local` hostname
- Works on any WiFi network (or direct AP mode)
- Zero configuration needed after setup

### Option 2: Broadcast UDP Discovery - Fallback
- ESP32 broadcasts UDP discovery request on local network
- Backend listens and responds with its IP:port
- Auto-connects to discovered backend
- Works within LAN segments

### Option 3: Direct AP Mode + Captive Portal
- ESP32 creates WiFi hotspot if backend not found
- User connects to ESP32's AP (e.g., "ChetanRobot_SETUP")
- Captive portal shows available backends on network
- User selects backend to connect

### Option 4: Configuration Portal + EEPROM
- ESP32 boots into AP mode first time
- Stores backend hostname/IP in EEPROM
- Reconnects using stored config on reboot
- User can trigger reconfig via GPIO button or command

## Implementation Tasks

### Task 1: Backend mDNS Advertising (Python FastAPI)
- Install `zeroconf` package
- Advertise FastAPI service as `_http._tcp` with hostname `chetan-robot`
- Include service info: port, device list, etc.

### Task 2: ESP32 mDNS Discovery + Connection
- Use ESP-IDF mDNS client to discover `chetan-robot.local`
- Implement fallback to broadcast UDP if mDNS fails
- Add retry mechanism with exponential backoff
- Store discovered IP temporarily

### Task 3: Broadcast UDP Discovery Service
- Implement UDP listener on backend port 5353 (or custom)
- Listen for discovery requests from ESP32
- Respond with backend IP:port
- Add to both firmware and backend

### Task 4: Fallback AP Mode + Web Configuration
- If backend not found within 30 seconds, create WiFi AP
- Implement captive portal showing network scan results
- Allow user to input custom IP or hostname
- Save to EEPROM for next boot

### Task 5: Configuration Persistence
- Add EEPROM storage for backend hostname/IP
- Implement reset mechanism (button press or command)
- Store WiFi credentials securely
- Add configuration validation

### Task 6: Testing & Documentation
- Test all 4 discovery methods
- Test failover scenarios
- Document setup process
- Add troubleshooting guide

## Architecture Diagram
```
ŌöīŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöÉ

## Expected Behavior After Implementation

title: Add SSM dynamics (S4, S5, Mamba) and drop-in SSMDynamics for TD-MPC2
body:
### Motivation
- Provide Phase 2 structured state-space world models (S4, S5, Mamba) so the TD-MPC2 dynamics can be swapped from an MLP to an SSM for stability, long-range memory, and rollout-error evaluation. 
- Expose the SSM variants as a drop-in replacement so downstream planning and Phase 3 work (MPPI, SimNorm, SAM) can be applied without changing trainer logic.

### Description
- Added an `ssm/` package with `s5_layer.py`, `s4_layer.py`, `mamba_layer.py`, and `ssm_world_model.py` implementing diagonal/stable recurrences and a `SSMDynamics` wrapper that projects state to latent space and manages hidden state via `reset_hidden` and `forward(z, a)`.
- Extended `TDMPC2Model` (`tdmpc2/model.py`) to accept `dynamics_type` and `ssm_state_dim`, instantiate either `MLPDynamics` or `SSMDynamics`, and reset SSM hidden state at the start of rollouts.
- Added rollout-error diagnostics to the trainer (`tdmpc2/trainer.py`) with new config fields `rollout_error_horizon` and `rollout_error_every_steps` and a helper `_compute_rollout_error_metric` that logs `rollout_error/h{H}` into metrics.
- Extended the CLI and entrypoints by adding `--dynamics-type` and `--ssm-state-dim` to `tdmpc2/train_tdmpc2.py`, created `train_tdmpc2_s4.py`, `train_tdmpc2_s5.py`, and `train_tdmpc2_mamba.py`, and exposed `tdmpc-s4`, `tdmpc-s5`, and `tdmpc-mamba` commands in `main.py`.

### Testing
- Compiled the codebase with `python -m compileall main.py tdmpc2 ssm train_tdmpc2_s4.py train_tdmpc2_s5.py train_tdmpc2_mamba.py`, which succeeded without syntax errors.
- Attempted a small runtime smoke test that constructs `TDMPC2Model` for each dynamics type and runs `rollout`, but this execution failed due to the environment missing `torch` (`ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'torch'`), so runtime validation could not be completed here.
- No other automated tests were added; existing training entrypoints and metrics logging were exercised via static checks and imports.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69d4d95f0354832e8b4fed5dcd5993c5)

title: v2: decision-engine statusbar rewrite
body:

- Complete rewrite from 4 bash scripts (~600 LOC) ŌåÆ single Python file (zero external deps)

- Runway alert only appears when you'll exhaust quota before it resets
- Context velocity arrows show how fast your context window is filling

title: Migrate to psycopg 3.x for Python 3.13 compatibility
body:
`psycopg2-binary` lacks Python 3.13 support, breaking Streamlit Cloud deployments.

## Changes

**Dependencies**
- `requirements.txt`: Replace `psycopg2-binary==2.9.9` ŌåÆ `psycopg[binary]==3.1.18`

**SQLAlchemy Dialect**
- `backend/app/core/config.py`: Update connection URL to use explicit driver dialect
```python
# Before
return f"postgresql://{self.POSTGRES_USER}:..."

# After  
return f"postgresql+psycopg://{self.POSTGRES_USER}:..."
```

**Import Path**
- `backend/app/db/database.py`: Fix relative import `from .config` ŌåÆ `from ..core.config`

**Docker Images**
- `deployment/docker/Dockerfile.{backend,frontend}`: Bump base image `python:3.11-slim` ŌåÆ `python:3.13-slim`

**Documentation**
- `README.md`: Note Python 3.13 support, update setup instructions

## Compatibility

Maintains backward compatibility with Python 3.11+. No API changes.

title: Migrate to async SQLAlchemy 2.0
body:
## Summary

- Migrated entire DB layer from synchronous SQLAlchemy (`create_engine` + `Session`) to async SQLAlchemy 2.0 (`create_async_engine` + `AsyncSession`)
- All ~60 route handlers, ~30 service functions, and 6 background pollers now use non-blocking async DB I/O
- Eliminates event loop starvation that caused 15-20 second intermittent page load delays (7 pollers were executing 10+ blocking DB calls/sec on a single-threaded uvicorn worker)

## Changes (31 files, +637/-608)

**Phase 0**: Converted 5 legacy `db.query().delete()` calls to SA 2.0 `delete()` statements; added `aiosqlite` dep

**Phase 1**: New async engine + session factory in `app/db.py` with `expire_on_commit=False`

**Phase 2**: Converted all route handlers and service functions ŌĆö mechanical `db.scalars()` ŌåÆ `await db.scalars()` transformation across 15 files

**Phase 3**: Replaced `asyncio.to_thread(SessionLocal())` pattern in all 6 pollers with native `async with AsyncSessionLocal()`

**Phase 4**: Converted 3 test files to `IsolatedAsyncioTestCase` with `aiosqlite`

**Phase 5**: Removed sync `engine` and `SessionLocal` from `app/db.py`; converted startup `create_all` to async

**Alembic**: Untouched ŌĆö creates its own sync engine via `engine_from_config()`, imports only `DATABASE_URL` and `Base`

## Test plan

- [x] `python -m compileall app tests alembic` ŌĆö all clean
- [x] Docker build succeeds
- [x] Container starts, all 7 pollers cycle without errors (10 nodes in 3.0s)
- [x] Page navigation dramatically faster ŌĆö no more 15-20s delays
- [x] All API endpoints return 200
- [x] Alembic `upgrade head` still works
- [ ] Run full test suite in Docker: `docker compose exec smp python -m unittest discover -s tests`

https://claude.ai/code/session_01C3MPb3Lm8aJbnPYDwM339H

title: Bump requests from 2.32.5 to 2.33.0
body:
Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.32.5 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • ­¤ōŻ Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. ­¤ōŻ

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

title: Add Torch 2 and NumPy 2 compatibility fixes
body:
Summary:
This ports the fork forward enough to run training and validation on a modern Python environment used by esmart-ai-models.

Changes:
- add `weights_only=False` to `torch.load()` call sites for PyTorch 2.6+
- replace deprecated `torch.cuda.amp.autocast(...)` usage with `torch.amp.autocast('cuda', ...)`
- replace removed NumPy aliases (`np.int`) with built-in `int`
- replace AP integration code to support NumPy 2.x (`np.trapezoid` with fallback)
- replace Pillow 10-removed `ImageFont.getsize()` calls with `getbbox()`

Files touched:
- `train.py`
- `utils/aws/resume.py`
- `utils/datasets.py`
- `utils/general.py`
- `utils/loggers/__init__.py`
- `utils/loss.py`
- `utils/metrics.py`
- `utils/plots.py`

Validation:
- training startup fixed under Python 3.12
- AP computation validated with NumPy 2.4.3
- patch rebased onto `experiments`, which is the direct descendant branch of the submodule-pinned commit used in esmart-ai-models

Release notes

2.20.0

  • New lexers:

    • Rell (#2914)

    • archetype: Fix catastrophic backtracking in GUID and ID patterns (#3064)

    • Java: Add module keywords (#2955)
    • Lean4: Add operators ]', ]?, ]! (#2946)
    • LESS: Support single-line comments (#3005)
    • LilyPond: Update to 2.25.29 (#2974)
    • LLVM: Support C-style comments (#3023, #2978)

title: Fix: Downgrade Python to 3.12 for pydantic compatibility
body:
## Problem

The build fails during dependency installation because Railpack defaults to Python 3.13, which is incompatible with pydantic-core 2.18.4 ŌĆö a transitive dependency of aiogram 3.7.0. PyO3, used to build pydantic-core's native extension, only supports up to Python 3.12, producing the error: "the configured Python interpreter version (3.13) is newer than PyO3's maximum supported version (3.12)".

## Solution

Added a railpack.toml at the repository root with `python = "3.12"` under the `[build]` section. This explicitly instructs Railpack to use Python 3.12 instead of the default 3.13, which is fully compatible with pydantic-core and all other aiogram dependencies.

### Changes
- **Created** `railpack.toml`

---
*Generated by [Railway](https://railway.com)*

title: Refactor cyclomatic complexity and resolve generic exceptions
body:
This PR addresses technical debt and improves code health by refactoring complex logic, fixing silent failures, and pruning redundant code artifacts, in accordance with the Scheduled Task: Code Health & Cleanup mission.

**Structural Health (The Architect)**
- Refactored `data/anki/security_check.py` to extract `_scan_tracked_file` and `_check_json_data`, significantly reducing the cyclomatic complexity of `main` and `check_file_for_private_data`.

**Resilience & Error Handling (The Sentinel)**
- Performed a silent failure audit across the codebase.
- Replaced bare `except:` clauses with targeted `except Exception:` or `except ImportError:` in:

All tests pass and formatting/linting is clean. No external API signatures were modified.

title: Add FastAPI weather backend with multi-source aggregation
body:
No backend existed in the repo. This adds a production-ready Python/FastAPI service that aggregates weather data from OpenWeatherMap and Open-Meteo, normalizes both sources to a common schema, and merges values via weighted averages for improved accuracy.

## Structure

- **`backend/main.py`** ŌĆō FastAPI app; `/health`, `/geocode`, `/weather` endpoints with CORS enabled
- **`backend/weather_service.py`** ŌĆō All aggregation logic: concurrent fetching, weighted merging, WMO code mapping, moto suitability scoring
- **`backend/requirements.txt`** ŌĆō `fastapi`, `uvicorn`, `httpx`, `python-dotenv`, `pydantic`
- **`backend/.env.example`** ŌĆō Env var template (`OPENWEATHERMAP_API_KEY`, `DEFAULT_CITY`, `PORT`)
- **`backend/README.md`** ŌĆō Endpoint docs, response schema, deployment guide
- **`backend/tests.py`** ŌĆō 17 offline unit tests covering helpers, merging logic, and scoring

## Key design points

- `/weather` accepts `city=`, `lat=`+`lon=`, or defaults to `Bucharest`
- Both sources fetched concurrently via `asyncio.gather`; OWM failures degrade gracefully (Open-Meteo always available, no key)
- **Moto score** (0ŌĆō100) factors precipitation, wind gusts, feels-like temp, and weather codes ŌåÆ label: `IDEAL / OK / ACCEPTABIL / RISCANT / EVIT─é`
- Response includes `current`, `daily` (7-day), and `hourly` (168h) blocks

```
GET /weather?city=Cluj-Napoca
ŌåÆ { city, latitude, longitude, timezone, current: { temperature, moto_score, moto_label, sources, ŌĆ” }, daily: [ŌĆ”], hourly: [ŌĆ”] }
```

Deploy with: `uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT`

title: Add CoreDNS DNS allowlist resolver to close DNS exfiltration channel
body:
## Summary

- Adds a CoreDNS service that intercepts all DNS traffic in the shared network namespace and only resolves domains in `allow-list.yaml`, returning `REFUSED` for everything else ŌĆö closing the DNS exfiltration channel that previously bypassed the mitmproxy allowlist
- Derives the CoreDNS zone allowlist from the same `allow-list.yaml` used by mitmproxy at build time, keeping HTTP and DNS policy in sync with no separate list to maintain
- Extends `entrypoint.sh` with DNAT rules redirecting all port-53 traffic to CoreDNS, explicit blocks for DoT (853), mDNS (5353), DoT-alt (8853), and a blanket `ip6tables OUTPUT REJECT` for IPv6

## Key files

- `infra/coredns/Dockerfile` ŌĆö multi-stage build: Python Alpine generates the Corefile, CoreDNS image bakes it in
- `infra/coredns/generate-corefile.py` ŌĆö reads `allow-list.yaml`, emits per-zone `forward` blocks with a catch-all `REFUSED` default
- `infra/coredns/test_generate_corefile.py` ŌĆö 46 tests, 100% coverage, verified in the `python:3.12-alpine` build container
- `infra/mitmproxy/entrypoint.sh` ŌĆö DNAT + port blocks + IPv6 reject
- `docker-compose.yml` ŌĆö `coredns` service; `COREDNS_UID` env var used consistently across both `coredns` and `mitmproxy` services

## Test plan

- [x] `cd infra/coredns && uv run pytest test_generate_corefile.py -v` ŌĆö all 46 pass locally
- [x] Container test: `docker build --target corefile-generator -f infra/coredns/Dockerfile -t coredns-test-env . && docker run --rm -v "$(pwd)/infra/coredns/test_generate_corefile.py":/work/test_generate_corefile.py coredns-test-env sh -c "pip install pytest pytest-cov --quiet --root-user-action=ignore && python -m pytest test_generate_corefile.py -v"` ŌĆö 46/46, 100% coverage
- [x] `docker compose config` ŌĆö validate compose file
- [x] Full devcontainer bring-up: `dig github.com` resolves, `dig evil.com` returns REFUSED, `nc -z -w2 1.1.1.1 853` fails

body:
Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • ­¤ōŻ Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. ­¤ōŻ

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

title: Merge setup.py into pyproject.toml
body:
Consolidates packaging configuration into `pyproject.toml`, removes `setup.cfg`, and simplifies `setup.py` to only the Cython/C extension build.

## Changes

- **`pyproject.toml`**: Added full `[project]` table with all metadata previously in `setup.py` ŌĆö name, version (`2.1.0`), description, author, license, classifiers, dependencies, homepage URL (`https://github.com/sequana/idr`), `requires-python = ">=3.9"`. Added `[tool.setuptools]` with `script-files = ["bin/idr"]` to preserve the CLI script. Added `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` from `setup.cfg`. The `readme = "README.md"` field ensures the README serves as the long description on PyPI, preserving the full project description previously in `setup.py`.
- **`setup.py`**: Reduced to extension-only ŌĆö retains Cython/C extension build logic (`idr.inv_cdf`) which cannot be expressed natively in `pyproject.toml` with the setuptools backend.
- **`setup.cfg`**: Removed; pytest config migrated to `pyproject.toml`.
- **`idr/__init__.py`**: `__version__` is now dynamically inferred at runtime using `importlib.metadata`, reflecting the version declared in `pyproject.toml`.
- **`README.md`**: Added a "Packaging" section documenting the migration from `setup.py`/`setup.cfg` to `pyproject.toml`, explaining what each file now contains and how the project description is preserved.

title: Refactor StructureAnalyzer to use iterative stack-based traversals
body:
This pull request addresses reliability and performance bottlenecks in `StructureAnalyzer` by migrating recursive file system traversals to stack-based iterative algorithms.

**Improvements:**
1. **Prevent Recursion Errors**: Deeply nested directories no longer risk hitting Python's recursion limit.
2. **Crash Prevention**: Added `try...except PermissionError` blocks when iterating over directories, preventing the entire analysis from crashing due to a single unreadable folder.
3. **Memory/Performance**: Iterative traversals generally incur less overhead than deeply nested function calls in Python.

The changes preserve the exact output structure and behavior (including the dictionary insertion order in `_build_tree`) while significantly hardening the analyzer against real-world repository anomalies.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [8812167526623986679](https://jules.google.com/task/8812167526623986679) started by @shenald-dev*

title: feat: Linked Data rebuild (v0.2.0)
body:
## Summary

CDS v0.2.0 converts every identity to a dereferenceable HTTP URI, making all events valid JSON-LD and achieving Tim Berners-Lee's 5-star Linked Data rating.

### Breaking changes
- `content_type` is now a URI string (was `CDSContentType` object)
- `source.@id` replaces `source.id`
- `integrity.signed_by` is now a full URI (`"https://signed-data.org"`)
- Events include `@context`, `@type`, `@id` fields
- Canonical bytes include `@context`/`@type`/`@id` (v0.1.0 events cannot be verified by v0.2.0 verifiers)

### What's new
- **Vocabulary layer:** 14 JSON-LD files (`vocab/`, `contexts/`) ŌĆö ontology, source registry, domain definitions
- **Python SDK:** `CDSVocab`, `CDSSources` URI constants, new JSON-LD envelope, lottery/weather ingestors ŌĆö **39 tests passing**
- **TypeScript SDK:** Mirror of Python vocab, new `CDSEvent` class with `@context`/`@type`/`@id` ŌĆö **21 tests passing, 0 type errors**
- **Documentation:** Spec v0.2.0, migration guide, Linked Data explainer, updated all existing docs

### 5-star Linked Data
| Stars | Criteria | CDS |
|---|---|---|
| :star: | Available online, open license | MIT |
| :star::star: | Structured data | JSON |
| :star::star::star: | Non-proprietary format | JSON |
| :star::star::star::star: | W3C standards | JSON-LD |
| :star::star::star::star::star: | Links to other data | URI identities |

## Test plan
- [x] Python: `cd sdk/python && pytest` ŌĆö 39 passed
- [x] TypeScript: `cd sdk/typescript && npm run typecheck && npm test` ŌĆö 21 passed, 0 errors
- [x] All 14 JSON-LD files validate with `jq`
- [x] End-to-end: construct event ŌåÆ `to_jsonld()` ŌåÆ verify `@context`/`@type`/`@id`
- [x] Signing roundtrip: sign ŌåÆ serialize ŌåÆ deserialize ŌåÆ verify

title: Initial HR Insights package with CLI, HTTP API, metrics, storage, docs, tests, and CI/docker tooling
body:
### Motivation

- Provide a baseline HR KPI calculation toolchain (CLI + HTTP API) to compute KPIs, hiring funnel, and data quality from employee CSV data.
- Add persistence and report history to support exports and retention management for operational usage.
- Include developer and CI ergonomics (packaging, Docker, Makefile, sample data, and automated tests) for easier iteration and validation.

### Description

- Add a new `hr_insights` package with modules: `models`, `metrics`, `hiring`, `quality`, `service`, `storage`, `server`, `cli`, and `config`, plus `__version__` in `__init__.py`.
- Implement KPI calculation and CSV parsing in `metrics.py`, hiring funnel in `hiring.py`, and data quality checks in `quality.py`, all wired by `service.py` for reuse by CLI and server.
- Provide a simple threaded HTTP API server in `server.py` with endpoints for `/kpis`, `/quality`, `/hiring-funnel`, `/reports`, `/reports/export`, `/reports/cleanup`, `/health`, `/ready`, and `/metrics`, along with API key auth, rate limiting, and report persistence using `storage.py` (SQLite).
- Add packaging metadata in `pyproject.toml` including console scripts `hr-kpi` and `hr-server`, a `Dockerfile`, `Makefile` shortcuts, a sample CSV dataset `data/sample_employees.csv`, a PRD `docs/PRD.md`, and an expanded `README.md` describing usage.
- Add GitHub Actions CI workflow `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, a `.gitignore`, and a comprehensive test suite under `tests/` covering CLI, config, metrics, hiring, quality, service, storage, and server integration scenarios.

### Testing

- Ran the unit and integration test suite with `pytest -q` (also exposed via `make test`) and the full suite completed successfully across the repository tests. 
- Server integration tests spawn the HTTP server and exercise `/kpis`, `/hiring-funnel`, report listing/export, API key enforcement, readiness probe, pagination validation, and cleanup and they passed. 
- Storage tests validated SQLite round-trips, CSV export, pagination, and date filtering and they passed. 
- Config and CLI tests (including conflicting CLI modes and JSON output) were executed and passed.

title: infra: add Makefile, Dockerfile, and docker-compose for Pillar stack
body:
Adds the build and container infrastructure to serve the HoloLang/Pillar stack ŌĆö C core compilation, Python runtime packaging, and multi-service orchestration.

## Makefile
- `.hl`-aware targets: `run`, `check FILE=`, `repl`, `info`, `skills`, `canvas`
- .NET-style lifecycle: `build` / `build-all` / `clean` / `publish`
- C Pillar core targets: `core` (builds `libbolo.a` + `libbolo.so` from `core/`), `core-clean`
- Docker targets: `docker-build`, `docker-up`, `docker-down`, `docker-run FILE=`, `docker-repl`, `docker-logs`
- CI convenience: `make ci` = `build + test`; `make all` = `build + core`

## Dockerfile
Multi-stage build:
- **`builder`** (`gcc:13`) ŌĆö compiles `core/**/*.c` ŌåÆ `libbolo.so`; no-op if C sources not yet present
- **`runtime`** (`python:3.12-slim`) ŌĆö installs HoloLang with all extras, copies compiled `.so`, runs as non-root `holonaut` user under `tini` init
- Exposes `50051` (gRPC), `8080` (WebSocket), `8000` (REST/Webhook)

## docker-compose.yml
Four services:

| Service | Image | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `pillar` | `hololang/pillar:latest` | Main runtime; runs `full_system.hl`; depends on `storage` healthy |
| `repl` | same | Profile-gated (`repl`); interactive shell_vm |
| `storage` | `ipfs/kubo:v0.28.0` | Offline+online distributed tensor storage; IPFS API on `:5001` |
| `grpc-proxy` | `envoyproxy/envoy:v1.29` | Profile-gated (`grpc-proxy`); HTTP/1.1 ŌåÆ gRPC transcoding |

Named volumes `pillar_data` and `ipfs_data` persist across restarts on a shared `pillar_net` bridge network.

title: Add Docker Compose and AGENTS.md for local development
body:
Adds a Docker Compose setup for local development (web + PostgreSQL) and an `AGENTS.md` to orient AI agents toward using it.

## Changes

- **`docker-compose.yml`** ŌĆö `web` (Django, port 8000) + `db` (Postgres 16) services; web waits on `db` health check, runs migrations on start, all env vars pre-configured
- **`Dockerfile`** ŌĆö Python 3.12-slim image; installs deps, runs `manage.py runserver`
- **`config/settings.py`** ŌĆö Database config now env-driven; defaults to SQLite (no change for existing non-Docker local dev), switches to PostgreSQL when `DB_ENGINE` is set
- **`requirements.txt`** ŌĆö Added `psycopg2-binary>=2.9`
- **`.env.example`** ŌĆö Documents all supported env vars
- **`AGENTS.md`** ŌĆö Quick-start guide for agents: `docker compose up --build`, exec commands, test invocation

## Quick start

```bash
docker compose up --build
# App at http://localhost:8000
```

title: Rewrite VM: Frame-based architecture, O(1) stack, 3.11-only, 41 differential tests
body:
Major architectural refactoring of the Python bytecode interpreter:

Stack discipline: Flip TOS from index 0 to index -1. All stack ops
are now O(1) ŌĆö append/pop instead of insert(0)/pop(0). Fixes pre-existing
unary op stack leak (insert without pop).

Frame-based execution: Introduce Frame class with explicit frame stack.
CALL pushes a new Frame, RETURN_VALUE pops and resumes caller. Eliminates
host recursion for guest calls ŌĆö guest call depth is no longer bounded by
Python's recursion limit.

VMFunction: Replace _mf_make() closure factory with VMFunction class that
stores code + defaults + closure metadata without creating a host callable.

Index-based fast locals: Frame.locals_fast is a list indexed by co_varnames
slot. LOAD_FAST/STORE_FAST use direct indexing, no name lookups.

IR via dis.get_instructions: Replace manual bytecode_optimize() with
dis.get_instructions(adaptive=False, show_caches=False). Handles
EXTENDED_ARG, CACHE slots, and jump target resolution automatically.

Correct 3.11 CALL protocol: _NULL sentinel (distinct from None) for
PUSH_NULL. CALL checks PEEK(argc+2) to determine method vs non-method
calls, matching CPython's ceval.c behavior.

Drop Python 2.7: Remove hasarg(), variable-width bytecode path, and all
2.7-only opcodes (PRINT_ITEM, SETUP_LOOP, CALL_FUNCTION variants, etc).

Exception handling: General try/except around opcode dispatch routes
exceptions through the 3.11 exception table.

Remove meta-circular hacks: No more py2vm name patching in STORE_NAME
or CALL. VMFunction/native callable distinction makes this unnecessary.

New opcodes: BUILD_SET, END_FOR, LIST_EXTEND, SET_UPDATE, SET_ADD,
LIST_TO_TUPLE, RAISE_VARARGS, BEFORE_WITH, DELETE_ATTR, DELETE_GLOBAL,
IMPORT_NAME, IMPORT_FROM, IMPORT_STAR. Tier 6 (generators/async) rejected
with NotImplementedError.

Testing: Add test_vm.py differential harness (41 tests) comparing VM
stdout against CPython across Tiers 1-5 ŌĆö arithmetic, functions, closures,
classes, exceptions, comprehensions, imports, f-strings, etc.

title: [Aikido] Fix security issue in python-multipart via minor version upgrade from 0.0.6 to 0.0.18
body:
Upgrade python-multipart to fix ReDoS vulnerability in Content-Type parsing and DoS from malformed multipart boundaries.
ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Breaking changes in this upgrade
All breaking changes by upgrading python-multipart from version 0.0.6 to 0.0.18 ([CHANGELOG](https://github.com/Kludex/python-multipart/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md)) | Version | Description | | --- | --- | | 
0.0.9
| Drop support for Python 3.7 | | 
0.0.13
| Rename import to `python_multipart` | | 
0.0.18
| Hard break if found data after last boundary on `MultipartParser` |
Ō£ģ 2 CVEs resolved by this upgrade
This PR will resolve the following CVEs: | Issue | Severity           | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | 
[CVE-2024-24762](https://app.aikido.dev/issues/25487533/detail?groupId=79519#CVE-2024-24762)
| 
HIGH
| [python-multipart] A ReDoS vulnerability in HTTP Content-Type header parsing allows attackers to send malicious form data that causes excessive CPU consumption and indefinite stalling, preventing the server from handling additional requests. | | 
[AIKIDO-2024-10493](https://app.aikido.dev/issues/25487533/detail?groupId=79519#AIKIDO-2024-10493)
| 
LOW
| [python-multipart] A maliciously crafted multipart request with junk data after the boundary causes resource exhaustion, leading to Denial of Service. The server becomes unresponsive and unable to process further requests. |

title: Handle floating RRULE UNTIL for local calendar events
body:
### Motivation
- When converting event `DTSTART`/`DTEND` datetimes to floating local time in `_parse_event`, a timezone-aware `RRULE` `UNTIL` can remain timezone-aware and cause naive/aware comparison errors in `dateutil` during recurrence expansion, so `UNTIL` must be normalized to match.

### Description
- Added logic in `_parse_event` to detect when `EVENT_RRULE` is a `Recur` and convert a timezone-aware `rrule.until` to a floating local datetime using `dt_util.as_local(...).replace(tzinfo=None)`.
- Added an inline comment explaining why `UNTIL` must be made floating to match a floating `DTSTART`.

### Testing
- Ran `python -m compileall homeassistant/components/local_calendar/calendar.py`, which could not complete in this environment because the pinned `pyenv` Python `3.14.2` is not installed, so compilation could not be verified.
- No other automated tests were executed in this environment.

------

title: Complete Python 3 migration and update build tooling to uv
body:
## Changes

- **Python 3 Migration**: Updated codebase for Python 3.12+ compatibility, including:
  - Fixed file object handling by separating read and write file descriptors (`rfd`/`wfd` and `rfile`/`wfile`)
  - Fixed dict iteration with `list(dict.items())` for Python 3 compatibility

- **Build Tooling**: Updated to use `uv` for dependency management and running commands
  - Added `.gitignore` for `.claude/` and `.venv/` directories
  - Updated README with `uv sync` setup instructions
  - Replaced all `python` commands with `uv run python` in examples

- **Documentation**: Updated README to reflect Python 3.12+ requirements and modern dependency versions (gevent 26.x, greenlet 3.x)

title: Bump requests from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0
body:
Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • ­¤ōŻ Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. ­¤ōŻ

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

New Contributors

  • @ŌĆŗM0d3v1 made their first contribution in psf/requests#6865
  • @ŌĆŗaminvakil made their first contribution in psf/requests#7220

  • @ŌĆŗmagsen made their first contribution in psf/requests#6553
  • @ŌĆŗRohan5commit made their first contribution in psf/requests#7227

Full Changelog: https://github.com/psf/requests/blob/main/HISTORY.md#2330-2026-03-25

v2.32.5

2.32.5 (2025-08-18)

Bugfixes

  • The SSLContext caching feature originally introduced in 2.32.0 has created a new class of issues in Requests that have had negative impact across a number of use cases. The Requests team has decided to revert this feature as long term maintenance of it is proving to be unsustainable in its current iteration.

Deprecations

  • Added support for Python 3.14.
  • Dropped support for Python 3.8 following its end of support.

title: Port Drupalgeddon.py to Python 3
body:
### Motivation
- Make the legacy `Drupalgeddon.py` script run under Python 3 by replacing Python 2-only APIs and addressing bytes/str differences used during hashing and HTTP requests.
- Fix password hash generation and base64 conversion so the Drupal password hashing helper works correctly with Python 3 byte digests.

### Description
- Updated shebang to `#!/usr/bin/env python3` and replaced `urllib2` with `urllib.request` and `urllib.error` for HTTP operations.
- Converted the `DrupalHash` helper to explicitly encode strings to bytes before hashing and adjusted the `custom64` routine to operate on byte values instead of using `ord` on str.
- Encoded the POST payload with `data=...encode('utf-8')`, decode HTTP responses safely with `.decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')`, and modernized print statements to Python 3 syntax.
- Minor refactors and naming cleanups including renaming `hash` to `password_hash`, `UA` to `ua`, and using `.format()` for the `post_data` construction.

### Testing
- Ran `python3 -m py_compile Drupalgeddon.py` which completed successfully.
- Executed `python3 Drupalgeddon.py` which ran under Python 3 and displayed usage/banner output as expected.

------

title: Bump python-dotenv from 1.2.1 to 1.2.2
body:
Bumps [python-dotenv](https://github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv) from 1.2.1 to 1.2.2.
Release notes

Sourced from python-dotenv's releases.

v1.2.2

Added

  • Support for Python 3.14, including the free-threaded (3.14t) build. (#)

Changed

  • The dotenv run command now forwards flags directly to the specified command by @ŌĆŗbbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#607
  • Improved documentation clarity regarding override behavior and the reference page.
  • Updated PyPy support to version 3.11.
  • Documentation for FIFO file support.
  • Support for Python 3.9.

Fixed

  • Improved set_key and unset_key behavior when interacting with symlinks by @ŌĆŗbbc2 in #790c5
  • Corrected the license specifier and added missing Python 3.14 classifiers in package metadata by @ŌĆŗJYOuyang in theskumar/python-dotenv#590

Breaking Changes

  • dotenv.set_key and dotenv.unset_key used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case. For that behavior to be restored in all cases, follow_symlinks=True should be used.

  • In the CLI, set and unset used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case.

  • dotenv.set_key, dotenv.unset_key and the CLI commands set and unset used to reset the file mode of the modified .env file to 0o600 in some situations. This is no longer the case: The original mode of the file is now preserved. Is the file needed to be created or wasn't a regular file, mode 0o600 is used.

Misc

  • skip 000 permission tests for root user by @ŌĆŗburnout-projects in theskumar/python-dotenv#561
  • Bump actions/checkout from 5 to 6 in the github-actions group by @ŌĆŗdependabot[bot] in theskumar/python-dotenv#593
  • Add Windows testing to CI by @ŌĆŗbbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#604
  • Improve workflow efficiency with best practices by @ŌĆŗtheskumar in theskumar/python-dotenv#609
  • Remove the use of sh in tests by @ŌĆŗbbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#612

New Contributors

  • @ŌĆŗJYOuyang made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#590
  • @ŌĆŗburnout-projects made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#561
  • @ŌĆŗcpackham-atlnz made their first contribution in theskumar/python-dotenv#597

Full Changelog: https://github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv/compare/v1.2.1...v1.2.2

Changelog

Sourced from python-dotenv's changelog.

[1.2.2] - 2026-03-01

Added

  • Support for Python 3.14, including the free-threaded (3.14t) build. (#588)

Changed

  • The dotenv run command now forwards flags directly to the specified command by [@ŌĆŗbbc2] in #607
  • Improved documentation clarity regarding override behavior and the reference page.
  • Updated PyPy support to version 3.11.
  • Documentation for FIFO file support.
  • Dropped Support for Python 3.9.

Fixed

  • Improved set_key and unset_key behavior when interacting with symlinks by [@ŌĆŗbbc2] in [790c5c0]
  • Corrected the license specifier and added missing Python 3.14 classifiers in package metadata by [@ŌĆŗJYOuyang] in #590

Breaking Changes

  • dotenv.set_key and dotenv.unset_key used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case. For that behavior to be restored in all cases, follow_symlinks=True should be used.

  • In the CLI, set and unset used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case.

  • dotenv.set_key, dotenv.unset_key and the CLI commands set and unset used to reset the file mode of the modified .env file to 0o600 in some situations. This is no longer the case: The original mode of the file is now preserved. Is the file needed to be created or wasn't a regular file, mode 0o600 is used.

title: Add IoT sensor failure prediction ML system
body:
Manufacturing equipment telemetry (temperature, vibration, pressure, RPM, voltage, error codes) requires a dedicated ML pipeline to predict machine failures before they occur. This adds a self-contained `src/iot/` module alongside the existing NYC Taxi pipeline.

## New module: `src/iot/`

- **`sensor_data.py`** ŌĆö validates raw telemetry: out-of-bounds clamp ŌåÆ NaN, median imputation for sensor drift/gaps, UTC timestamp coercion, `add_failure_label()` that looks ahead `horizon_minutes` per machine, synthetic data generator for tests/demos
- **`features.py`** ŌĆö per-machine rolling feature engineering over 10-min / 30-min / 1-h windows:
  - rolling mean, std, min, max, variance
  - OLS gradient (trend) on 10-min window
  - spike indicator: `value > ╬╝ + 3Žā` within window
  - cyclic hour encoding (sin/cos), error code lag + rolling count
- **`train.py`** ŌĆö trains Random Forest or Gradient Boosting with MLflow logging, saves `.pkl` + feature schema JSON; ships a CLI entry point

```bash
# Train on real data
python -m src.iot.train --data sensors.csv --algo rf

# Or use built-in synthetic data
python -m src.iot.train --algo gb
```

## API: `/predict/sensor-failure`

New POST endpoint on `api/main.py` accepting a batch of `SensorReading` objects. Returns `failure_probability` + `will_fail` flag per reading, with a heuristic fallback (error-code-based score) when no trained model is present. Prometheus metrics wired in.

```json
POST /predict/sensor-failure
{"readings": [{"timestamp": "2026-03-12T11:05:21Z", "machine_id": "machine_27",
               "temperature": 84.3, "vibration": 0.02, "pressure": 101.3,
               "rpm": 3500, "voltage": 220, "error_code": 0}]}
```

title: v0.3 Memory Matures: Tier 2 complete (F7-F12)
body:
## Summary

Ships the complete Tier 2 milestone in one drop ŌĆö 6 architectural features, 178 new tests, 0 new pip deps, full backward compat.

| # | Feature | Key modules |
|---|---------|-------------|
| F7 | 4-tier memory consolidation w/ decay | `memory/consolidator.py`, `memory/decay.py` |
| F8 | Contradiction detection + supersession | `memory/contradiction.py`, `store.record_decision_checked` |
| F9 | Time-travel snapshots | `snapshots/manager.py`, `scripts/snapshot-guard.py` |
| F10 | Learned workspace profiles | `memory/profiler.py` |
| F11 | Cross-workspace pattern detection | `memory/pattern_detector.py`, `scripts/pattern-cron.py` |
| F12 | Inter-workspace signals (pub/sub) | `memory/signals.py` |

### Shared primitives (new)
`migration.py` (safe ALTER helper), `decay.py` (Ebbinghaus), `query_helpers.py` (cosine + top-k), `EmbeddingService.embed_batch_from_db`.

### Schema changes (all backward compatible)
- `sessions.memory_tier` (working/episodic)
- `decisions.contradiction_score`
- new `semantic_memories`, `procedural_memories` tables
- new `signals` table in `meta.db`
- All via `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` + OperationalError catch; existing dbs migrate on first open.

### Integration
- **session-preamble**: snapshot on auto-switch; injects semantic facts, workspace profile, pending signals
- **session-end**: `maybe_compact()`, profile regen when stale, fires `pattern-cron.py` weekly
- **memory-bridge**: new `signal` / `read_signals` event types; decision handler now uses `record_decision_checked`
- **hooks.json**: new PreToolUse matcher for `Write` -> `snapshot-guard.py`

### New user skills
`compact-memory`, `decision-history`, `rollback`, `profile-refresh`, `cross-patterns`, `emit-signal`

### Test results
60 -> **238 passing, 0 failing**. Zero regressions.

### Build method
Multi-agent parallel build: Wave 1 (foundation) sequential -> Wave 2 (6 features in parallel) -> Wave 3 (sequential integration) -> Wave 4 (verify + ship).

## Test plan
- [x] `python -m pytest tests/ -q` -> 238 passed
- [x] `python -c "import json; json.load(open('hooks/hooks.json'))"` valid
- [x] All new modules import cleanly
- [ ] Human smoke: run a real session, verify PreCompact + snapshot + signal flows end-to-end
- [ ] Verify migration on an existing v0.2 memory.db (auto ALTER should be a no-op on re-open)

## Follow-ups (not in this PR)
- Tier 3 features (F13-F20) ŌĆö session threads, billing, weekly summaries, environments, vault, deploy linking, inbox, NL router
- Port v0.3 into the local runtime `C:\Tentaqles\.tentaqles\` (still pinned to v0.2)
- LLM-based contradiction confirmation (full scope of F8)
- Weekly cron scheduling for `pattern-cron.py` and `compaction-cron.py`

title: feat: CRUD for documents, components, milestones, and templates
body:
## Summary

Extends the CLI with four new entity types, each with its own storage quirks.

- `huly documents list/get/create/update/describe` ŌĆö documents live in teamspaces (not projects); content is a blob ref accessed via Collaborator RPC with field `"content"`
- `huly components list/get/create/update` ŌĆö project-scoped, optional `--lead` with fuzzy person match
- `huly milestones list/get/create/update` ŌĆö project-scoped, `--target-date YYYY-MM-DD` parsing, status enum (planned/in-progress/completed)
- `huly templates list/get/describe` ŌĆö descriptions are inline ProseMirror JSON (not blob refs), so reads/writes go through `TxUpdateDoc` directly; safety check prevents corruption if a blob ref is detected
- Collaborator RPC generalized to `get_content(class_id, object_id, field, blob_ref)` ŌĆö works for any entity, not just issues

## Context

- Template `describe --set` includes a safety check: if the description field looks like a blob ref (`*-description-*` pattern), it aborts rather than corrupting data by overwriting the ref with raw JSON
- `get_description`/`set_description` remain as convenience wrappers for backward compatibility
- Components and milestones are currently empty in the EFS workspace but the API classes are verified queryable

title: Bump psycopg2 from 2.6.1 to 2.9.11
body:
Bumps [psycopg2](https://github.com/psycopg/psycopg2) from 2.6.1 to 2.9.11.
Changelog

Sourced from psycopg2's changelog.

Current release

What's new in psycopg 2.9.11 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

  • Add support for Python 3.14.
  • Avoid a segfault passing more arguments than placeholders if Python is built with assertions enabled (:ticket:[#1791](https://github.com/psycopg/psycopg2/issues/1791)).
  • Add riscv64 platform binary packages (:ticket:[#1813](https://github.com/psycopg/psycopg2/issues/1813)).
  • ~psycopg2.errorcodes map and ~psycopg2.errors classes updated to PostgreSQL 18.
  • Drop support for Python 3.8.

What's new in psycopg 2.9.10 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

  • Add support for Python 3.13.
  • Receive notifications on commit (:ticket:[#1728](https://github.com/psycopg/psycopg2/issues/1728)).
  • ~psycopg2.errorcodes map and ~psycopg2.errors classes updated to PostgreSQL 17.
  • Drop support for Python 3.7.

What's new in psycopg 2.9.9 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

  • Add support for Python 3.12.
  • Drop support for Python 3.6.

What's new in psycopg 2.9.8 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

  • Wheel package bundled with PostgreSQL 16 libpq in order to add support for recent features, such as sslcertmode.

What's new in psycopg 2.9.7 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

  • Fix propagation of exceptions raised during module initialization (:ticket:[#1598](https://github.com/psycopg/psycopg2/issues/1598)).
  • Fix building when pg_config returns an empty string (:ticket:[#1599](https://github.com/psycopg/psycopg2/issues/1599)).
  • Wheel package bundled with OpenSSL 1.1.1v.

What's new in psycopg 2.9.6 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

title: Bump requests from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0
body:
Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • ­¤ōŻ Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. ­¤ōŻ

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

New Contributors

  • @ŌĆŗM0d3v1 made their first contribution in psf/requests#6865
  • @ŌĆŗaminvakil made their first contribution in psf/requests#7220
  • @ŌĆŗE8Price made their first contribution in psf/requests#6960
  • @ŌĆŗmitre88 made their first contribution in psf/requests#7244
  • @ŌĆŗmagsen made their first contribution in psf/requests#6553
  • @ŌĆŗRohan5commit made their first contribution in psf/requests#7227

Full Changelog: https://github.com/psf/requests/blob/main/HISTORY.md#2330-2026-03-25

v2.32.5

2.32.5 (2025-08-18)

Bugfixes

  • The SSLContext caching feature originally introduced in 2.32.0 has created a new class of issues in Requests that have had negative impact across a number of use cases. The Requests team has decided to revert this feature as long term maintenance of it is proving to be unsustainable in its current iteration.

Deprecations

  • Added support for Python 3.14.
  • Dropped support for Python 3.8 following its end of support.
Changelog

Sourced from requests's changelog.

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • ­¤ōŻ Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. ­¤ōŻ

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

2.32.5 (2025-08-18)

Bugfixes

  • The SSLContext caching feature originally introduced in 2.32.0 has created a new class of issues in Requests that have had negative impact across a number of use cases. The Requests team has decided to revert this feature as long term maintenance of it is proving to be unsustainable in its current iteration.

Deprecations

  • Added support for Python 3.14.
  • Dropped support for Python 3.8 following its end of support.
Commits
  • bc04dfd v2.33.0
  • 66d21cb Merge commit from fork
  • 8b9bc8f Move badges to top of README (#7293)
  • e331a28 Remove unused extraction call (#7292)

  • ebf7190 Bump github/codeql-action from 4.32.0 to 4.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • ­¤ōŻ Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. ­¤ōŻ

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

Bugfixes

  • The SSLContext caching feature originally introduced in 2.32.0 has created a new class of issues in Requests that have had negative impact across a number of use cases. Deprecations

title: Add Wikipedia command, structured logging, proper error handling, and message splitting to GopalBot
body:
GopalBot used a bare `discord.Client` with `print()` logging, silent 2000-char response truncation, no prefix commands, and no Wikipedia support. This rewrites `bot.py` to a production-ready structure.

## Core changes

- **`discord.ext.commands.Bot`** ŌĆö migrated from raw client; all features now use `@bot.command` decorators with docstrings surfaced via `!help`
- **`!wiki <query>`** ŌĆö Wikipedia integration returning a 3-sentence summary as a rich embed (title, URL, footer); handles `DisambiguationError` and `PageError` with user-friendly messages
- **`!ask <question>`** ŌĆö explicit prefix command for Grok AI queries, decoupled from mention/name/DM triggers (which are preserved)
- **Message splitting** ŌĆö replaces `reply[:1997] + "..."` with `split_message()` that breaks on newlines and sends multiple messages
- **Structured logging** ŌĆö `logging.basicConfig` with timestamp + level + logger name, replacing all `print()` calls
- **Error handling** ŌĆö `on_command_error` for `MissingRequiredArgument` (names the missing param), `CommandNotFound` (echoes the attempted command), and a generic fallback; env var validation raises `EnvironmentError` at startup if either token is absent
- **Bot presence** ŌĆö sets "Listening to !help" on `on_ready`
- **Case-insensitive name stripping** ŌĆö uses `re.sub(..., flags=re.IGNORECASE)` instead of two sequential `.replace()` calls

## Files changed

- `bot.py` ŌĆö full rewrite
- `requirements.txt` ŌĆö adds `wikipedia==1.4.0`
- `README.md` ŌĆö updated feature list, commands table, dependencies
- `.gitignore` ŌĆö added (excludes `__pycache__`, `*.pyc`, `.env`)

```python
# Before: silent truncation
if len(reply) > 2000:
    reply = reply[:1997] + "..."

# After: split at newlines, send multiple messages
def split_message(text: str, limit: int = DISCORD_MAX_LENGTH) -> list[str]:
    chunks = []
    while len(text) > limit:
        split_at = text.rfind("\n", 0, limit)
        if split_at == -1:
            split_at = limit
        chunks.append(text[:split_at])
        text = text[split_at:].lstrip("\n")
    if text:
        chunks.append(text)
    return chunks
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

The user has provided their current bot.py code (shown in image ![image1](image1)) and wants a full improved version with the following enhancements:

1. **Add Wikipedia API integration** - Add a `!wiki` command to search Wikipedia and return summaries
2. **Improve code structure** - Use discord.ext.commands with proper command decorators
3. **Better error handling** - Implement proper exception handling throughout
4. **Add logging** - Add logging for debugging and monitoring
5. **Code organization** - Separate concerns into functions/methods where appropriate
6. **Add more commands** - Include the existing Grok AI functionality but organized better
7. **Improve the Grok AI response** - Better formatting and error handling for AI responses
8. **Message truncation** - Properly handle long responses (current code truncates at 2000 chars)
9. **Add help command** - Built-in help for available commands
10. **Configuration** - Clean separation of configuration from code

Current features to preserve:
- Discord bot using discord.py
- Grok AI integration for chat completions
- Bot responds to mentions and DMs
- Message logging

Return a complete, production-ready bot.py file with all these improvements that the user can directly replace their current file with.

The following is the prior conversation context from the user's chat exploration (may be truncated):

User: WHAT DO I click after i enter variables in railway to add discord key and grok key

Assistant: I need to search for information about how to add environment variables (Discord key and Grok key) in Railway.

Assistant: After entering your variables in Railway, here's what to click:

## To Add Discord Key and Grok Key in Railway:

1. **Open your project** in Railway
2. **Click "Variables"** (in the left sidebar under your project settings)
3. **Click the "+" button** or **"New Variable"** button
4. **Enter the variable names and values:**
   - **Key:** `DISCORD_KEY` ŌåÆ **Value:** (your Discord bot token)
   - **Key:** `GROK_KEY` ŌåÆ **Value:** (your Grok API key)
5. **Click "Save"** or the checkmark icon Ō£ō

Railway will **automatically restart your project** after you save the variables.

---

### In Your Code
Access them like this:
```javascript
const discordKey = process.env.DISCORD_KEY;
const grokKey = process.env.GROK_KEY;
```

**Note:** Make sure your variable names match exactly what you reference in your code (they're case-sensitive).

Assistant: Perfect! I can see your Railway Variables panel. Here's exactly what to do:

## To Add Your Discord Key and Grok Key:

1. **You're already in the right place** - the "Variables" tab is open Ō£ō

2. **For the Discord Token:**
   - The key field already shows `DISCORD_TOKEN` (you can change it to `DISCORD_KEY` if you prefer)
   - Click in the **"VALUE or ${REF}"** field
   - Paste your Discord bot token
   - Click the **purple "Add" button** Ō£ō

3. **For the Grok Key:**
   - After adding the Discord token, you'll see a new row
   - In the key field, type: `GROK_KEY`
   - Click in the **"VALUE or ${REF}"** field
   - Paste your Grok API key
   - Click the **purple "Add" button** Ō£ō

---

**That's it!** After you click **"Add"**, Railway will:

The asterisks (`*******`) indicate that Railway is securely hiding your sensitive keys for privacy.

---

## What Happens Next:

  ```javascript

## You're All Set! ­¤Äē

title: Bump pyasn1 from 0.4.2 to 0.6.3
body:
Bumps [pyasn1](https://github.com/pyasn1/pyasn1) from 0.4.2 to 0.6.3.
Release notes

Sourced from pyasn1's releases.

Release 0.6.3

It's a minor release.

  • Added nesting depth limit to ASN.1 decoder to prevent stack overflow from deeply nested structures (CVE-2026-30922).
  • Fixed OverflowError from oversized BER length field.
  • Fixed DeprecationWarning stacklevel for deprecated attributes.
  • Fixed asDateTime incorrect fractional seconds parsing.

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

Release 0.6.2

It's a minor release.

  • Fixed continuation octet limits in OID/RELATIVE-OID decoder (CVE-2026-23490).
  • Added support for Python 3.14.
  • Added SECURITY.md policy.
  • Migrated to pyproject.toml packaging.

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

Release 0.6.1

It's a minor release.

  • Added support for Python 3.13.
  • Cleaned Python 2-related code.
  • Removed bdist_wheel universal flag from setup.cfg.

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

Release 0.6.0

It's a major release where we drop Python 2 support entirely. The most significant changes are:

  • Removed support for EOL Python 2.7, 3.6, 3.7
  • Added support for previously missing RELATIVE-OID construct
  • Updated link to Layman's Guide

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

Release 0.5.1

It's a minor release.

  • Added support for PyPy 3.10 and Python 3.12
  • Updated RTD configuration to include a dummy index.rst redirecting to contents.html, ensuring compatibility with third-party documentation and search indexes.
  • Fixed the API breakage wih decoder.decode(substrateFun=...). A substrateFun passed to decoder.decode() can now be either v0.4 Non-Streaming or v0.5 Streaming. pyasn1 will detect and handle both cases transparently. A substrateFun passed to one of the new streaming decoders is still expected to be v0.5 Streaming only.

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

... (truncated)

Changelog

Sourced from pyasn1's changelog.

Revision 0.6.3, released 16-03-2026

  • CVE-2026-30922 (GHSA-jr27-m4p2-rc6r): Added nesting depth limit to ASN.1 decoder to prevent stack overflow from deeply nested structures (thanks for reporting, romanticpragmatism)
  • Fixed OverflowError from oversized BER length field [issue #54](pyasn1/pyasn1#54) [pr #100](pyasn1/pyasn1#100)
  • Fixed DeprecationWarning stacklevel for deprecated attributes [issue #86](pyasn1/pyasn1#86) [pr #101](pyasn1/pyasn1#101)
  • Fixed asDateTime incorrect fractional seconds parsing [issue #81](pyasn1/pyasn1#81) [pr #102](pyasn1/pyasn1#102)

Revision 0.6.2, released 16-01-2026

  • CVE-2026-23490 (GHSA-63vm-454h-vhhq): Fixed continuation octet limits in OID/RELATIVE-OID decoder (thanks to tsigouris007)
  • Added support for Python 3.14 [pr #97](pyasn1/pyasn1#97)
  • Added SECURITY.md policy
  • Fixed unit tests failing due to missing code [issue #91](pyasn1/pyasn1#91) [pr #92](pyasn1/pyasn1#92)
  • Migrated to pyproject.toml packaging [pr #90](pyasn1/pyasn1#90)

Revision 0.6.1, released 10-09-2024

  • Added support for Python 3.13 and updated GitHub Actions [pr #73](pyasn1/pyasn1#73)
  • Removed Python 2 support and related code [pr #62](pyasn1/pyasn1#62) [pr #61](pyasn1/pyasn1#61) [pr #60](pyasn1/pyasn1#60)
  • Improved error handling and consistency [pr #71](pyasn1/pyasn1#71) [pr #70](pyasn1/pyasn1#70)

  • Cleaned documentation and comments [pr #63](pyasn1/pyasn1#63)
  • Removed bdist_wheel universal flag from setup.cfg [pr #69](pyasn1/pyasn1#69)

title: Bump protobuf from 6.33.2 to 6.33.5 in /python
body:
Bumps [protobuf](https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf) from 6.33.2 to 6.33.5.
Release notes

Sourced from protobuf's releases.

Protocol Buffers v34.0-rc1

Announcements

  • This version includes breaking changes to: C++, Objective-C, PHP, Python.
  • [Bazel] Remove deprecated ProtoInfo.transitive_imports. Use equivalent transitive_sources instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/0a5c2f6b633c1e5259f566cb42d30fe347b8aadb)
  • [C++] Make generator headers private (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/3a2af3510f0d454dbe3e4dc281674b61c4d20b9e)
  • [C++] Add a debug check that the target of CopyFrom is not a descendant of the source. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/7a7589823d2cfaaf7994b050e98d5d553bc9b1c1)
  • [C++] Add [[nodiscard]] to many APIs. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/a70115f33f9af2c4b2202c800b84837e7fe0d738)
  • [C++] Make the arena-enabled constructors of RepeatedField, RepeatedPtrField, and Map private. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/ef890c3d0c79398c70e047fe5dd893f460ba2336)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::label() in OSS. Use is_repeated() or is_required() instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b76faa921fdd244f374c7be0bddd4050fc42c292)
  • [C++] Removes proto2::util::MessageDifferencer::AddIgnoreCriteria that takes a raw pointer as an argument in favor of the overload that takes a unique_ptr. Remove macro PROTOBUF_FUTURE_REMOVE_ADD_IGNORE_CRITERIA (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b115358c64127896fed88b8b5ef5d91d86d8cbae)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::has_optional_keyword() in OSS. Use is_repeated() or has_presence() instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/68346ec9348e932664e58c3ecdcd1478f95233a8)
  • [C++] Remove AddUnusedImportTrackFile() and ClearUnusedImportTrackFiles(). Remove PROTOBUF_FUTURE_RENAME_ADD_UNUSED_IMPORT (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/837a2cd1d6c75402b2503ffe7cd8aeaf25868536)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::is_optional() in OSS. Use (!is_required() && !is_repeated()) instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/9dbc5d479a8e453921485d8d3de47fb3c005f1af)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated UseDeprecatedLegacyJsonFieldConflicts() (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/c301c2ca286327a21c50c0c4cd877afc9c655b00)
  • [C++] All entity names have length limit (2afb0dc)
  • [ObjC] Remove generate_minimal_imports generation option warning (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/45b1297fdaad5a9436d0e207422168c38dc45ac4)
  • [ObjC] Fix nullability annotations on some GPB*Dictionary types. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/ea67d6d26a48478a567c404679e3bb99cf230d50)
  • [ObjC] Remove -[GPBFieldDescriptor optional] (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/3414dc151eb4dcbdb2ca952e2589993bf7af75c4)
  • [Other] Remove deprecated flag for enabling MSVC support (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/97c979be6e0907e1051bee62584dac4594e73fa7)
  • [PHP] Remove deprecated PHP APIs (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/9c45014099a4f7004fab6dd1278de2f4f2a393c5)
  • [PHP] Remove deprecated PHP APIs FieldDescriptor getLabel, use IsRepeated or isRequired instead. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/42081219920c6fad17ba6ddd1e28d111bcfb3345, https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/cd76e675b14d00dda5623b30835d2bc7105fccc6, https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/42081219920c6fad17ba6ddd1e28d111bcfb3345)
  • [PHP] Add PHP typehints for setters and remove redundant GPBUtil checks (protocolbuffers/protobuf#25296) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/aee03b78929c02461a5f9d8e136a2a016359b0cd)
  • [PHP] support default values for editions/proto2 (protocolbuffers/protobuf#25161) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b01099d56350551bae3da88b97bf3027274c9f17)
  • [Python] Raise errors in OSS when assign bool to int/enum field in Python Proto. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/5b116fe2f14f49dd0cc3b76089983717f211025c)
  • [Python] Remove float_format/double_format from python proto text_format (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/e4854a186e0bfa867d5bfa5cd850608a948fd488)
  • [Python] Raise TypeError when convert non-timedelta to Duration, or convert non-datetime to Timestamp in python proto. (Original code may raise ArributeError) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/00aaca1b4d98954bc2933d7c8a5379ba6088124c)
  • [Python] Remove float_precision from python proto json_format (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/f027f1fcd52b9d080b7ee79f4024f53cf54e0dc5)
  • [Python] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::label() in OSS. Use is_repeated() or is_required() instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b76faa921fdd244f374c7be0bddd4050fc42c292)
  • [Python] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor.label (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/0a8ff55518ea5874478ad5b26515b31d186045a9)
  • [Python] Remove deprecated UseDeprecatedLegacyJsonFieldConflicts() (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/c301c2ca286327a21c50c0c4cd877afc9c655b00)
  • Protobuf News may include additional announcements or pre-announcements for upcoming changes.
  • Migration Guide may include additional guidance for breaking changes.

Bazel

  • Fix: cc_toolchain should prefer protoc when prebuilt flag is flipped. (#25168) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/8c857c3a1c6a106b0a096f1c9fa504bfaca035a9)
  • Breaking change: Remove deprecated ProtoInfo.transitive_imports. Use equivalent transitive_sources instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/0a5c2f6b633c1e5259f566cb42d30fe347b8aadb)
  • Feat(bazel): wire up prebuilt protoc toolchain (#24115) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/cc23698b486e690ea2eb873cc7596a87c74a3ba6)
  • Migrate proto_descriptor_set (#23369) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/8d4dfdd39a7a242a9ed631a6ab2192c57dd9b9c8)

Compiler

  • Ruby codegen: support generation of rbs files (#15633) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/6ebdf851ba78728f0aa145d38454ed9a316fb08d)
  • Avoid collision name problems between a message named Xyz and a direct sibling enum named XyzView (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/eba53e8f172b273d679759a72ce4250131ee3df1)
  • Generalizing and implementing ValidateFeatureSupport for both Options and Features during proto parsing (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/ed3c57114d8e2b47cca7697ddaa50c1b3762a6b0)
  • Fix a bug with custom features outside of the pb package. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/872d3ce7a4da00d7dcec33ced20cfe45235935e8)
  • Fix import option handling when include_imports isn't set. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/9ef9e80afd9bc8379d578fe67e5ab0738728c04e)
  • Fix a bug in STRICT check of namespaced enums to properly check for 'reserved 1 to max' (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/1229d4adba24c0952ab85ce96bc7b7f8a1fe6d0f)
  • Prevent accidental stripping of debug_redact options via import option. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/f58b098bffa7ca4045ef7773b09151a6af5d0c28)

C++

  • Add EnumerateEnumValues function. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/397d5d99db274b379d1384814074bf7df39d32f7)

... (truncated)
