title: feat: add Prometheus metrics and Grafana monitoring
body:
## Summary
- Add `prometheus_client` dependency and expose `/metrics` Prometheus endpoint on port 8201
- Instrument all MCP tool functions, upload processing, RAG/LLM helpers, and MinIO storage operations with counters, histograms, and gauges
- 12 new tests covering metric increments across all instrumented layers (27 total tests pass)

## Metrics exposed
- `pageindex_tool_calls_total`, `pageindex_tool_errors_total`, `pageindex_tool_duration_seconds` (by tool)
- `pageindex_uploads_total` (by status), `pageindex_upload_duration_seconds`, `pageindex_active_uploads`
- `pageindex_rag_searches_total`, `pageindex_rag_duration_seconds`, `pageindex_llm_calls_total`, `pageindex_llm_duration_seconds`
- `pageindex_minio_operations_total`, `pageindex_minio_duration_seconds` (by operation)
- `pageindex_documents_total`

## Infra changes (separate repo)
Prometheus scrape job and Grafana dashboard JSON already added to `hetzner-deployment-service` configmap.

## Test plan
- [x] All 27 tests pass (`uv run pytest tests/ -v`)
- [x] All 14 custom metric names present in `/metrics` output
- [x] `uv lock --check` confirms lock file is up to date
- [ ] Deploy to staging and verify Prometheus scrapes `/metrics` successfully
- [ ] Verify Grafana dashboard loads with live data

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Adds end-to-end Prometheus metrics for PageIndex MCP and exposes a `/metrics` endpoint on port 8201 for scraping. Integrates with the existing Grafana stack for visibility into tools, uploads, RAG/LLM, and MinIO.

- **New Features**
  - Expose `/metrics` via `prometheus_client` on the Starlette app.
  - Instrumentation added for: MCP tools (calls, errors, duration), uploads (status, duration, active), RAG/LLM (calls/searches, duration), MinIO (ops, duration), and a documents gauge.
  - 12 new tests for metric increments; all 27 tests pass.
  - Implementation plan and design spec added under `docs/superpowers/`. Prometheus scrape job and Grafana dashboard JSON are provisioned in the infra repo.

- **Dependencies**
  - Added `prometheus_client` to `pyproject.toml`.

<sup>Written for commit ea7cdb42a3108c03070ab6dc8415a972ae639342. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

title: feat: add extra_commands support to presets with odoo_version markers
body:
We have to handle a particular packaging case for old odoo.
- `vatnumber` is needed for odoo <= 13.0, see [here](https://github.com/odoo/odoo/blob/13.0/requirements.txt#L40)
- `vatnumber` package needs `2to3` to build which has been removed from recent setuptools
- in the past we had pinned "setuptools<58" as a workaround:
   - in [oca ci](https://github.com/OCA/maintainer-quality-tools/pull/695)
   - in [emoi](https://gitlab.trobz.com/packages/emoi/-/blob/py3/emoi/utils/helper.py#L322)
- then on oca ci, the workaround was moved to a dedicated container for legacy odoo: see [here](https://github.com/OCA/oca-ci/pull/98)

This PR is an attempt at handling it properly in odoo-venv: 

## Summary
- Add `extra_commands` field to presets.toml to run additional commands at specific stages during venv creation
- Commands can be conditional based on `odoo_version` markers (e.g., `odoo_version <= '13.0'`)
- Support for environment variables in commands (e.g., `UV_NO_BUILD_ISOLATION=1`)
- Verbose output shows condition and environment for each executed command

## Details
The new `extra_commands` feature allows running arbitrary commands at three stages:
- `after_venv` - after virtual environment creation
- `after_requirements` - after installing all requirements
- `after_odoo_install` - after installing Odoo in editable mode

Each command can have:
- `command`: list of command arguments
- `when`: marker expression (supports `odoo_version` and standard packaging markers)
- `stage`: one of the three stages above
- `env`: optional dict of environment variables

## Concretely for `vatnumber` 
```toml
[[common.extra_commands]]
command = ["uv", "pip", "install", "setuptools<58.0"]
when = "odoo_version <= '13.0'"
stage = "after_requirements"

[[common.extra_commands]]
command = ["uv", "pip", "install", "vatnumber==1.2"]
when = "odoo_version <= '13.0'"
stage = "after_requirements"
env = { UV_NO_BUILD_ISOLATION = "1" }
```

title: feat: auto-handle packages requiring --no-build-isolation
body:
## Summary

Moves hardcoded Odoo compatibility workarounds from `presets.toml` into the core `create_odoo_venv` logic, so they apply automatically regardless of preset.

### `_NO_BUILD_ISOLATION_PACKAGES`

A new dict mapping package names to an optional when-marker. When any such package is found in **any** requirement source (Odoo's `requirements.txt`, addons dirs, user sources, manifests) and its when-marker evaluates to True, it is automatically:
- excluded from the batch `uv pip install -r` run
- installed afterwards with `--no-build-isolation`

| Package | Condition |
|---|---|
| `vatnumber` | `odoo_version <= '13.0'` |
| `suds-jurko` | `odoo_version <= '13.0'` |
| `rfc6266-parser` | always (when present in any source) |

### Legacy build tools for Odoo <= 13.0

`setuptools<58.0` and `wheel` are now installed automatically when `odoo_version <= '13.0'` (setuptools 58 dropped 2to3 support required by vatnumber and suds-jurko). Runs before the `--no-build-isolation` installs so the build tools are ready.

### Preset cleanup

Removes from `presets.toml [common]`:
- `vatnumber` and `suds-jurko` from `ignore_from_odoo_requirements`
- `extra_commands` entries for vatnumber, suds-jurko, and `setuptools<58.0 wheel`

title: Bump requests from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0 in the pip group across 1 directory
body:
Bumps the pip group with 1 update in the / directory: [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests). Updates `requests` from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • ­¤ōŻ Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. ­¤ōŻ

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

New Contributors

title: Fix Django admin static files and login 500 on Vercel
body:
Django admin renders unstyled on Vercel because `collectstatic` output (`staticfiles/`) is never bundled into the Lambda ŌĆö WhiteNoise has nothing to serve. Additionally, `migrate` never ran on deploy, causing a 500 on `POST /api/auth/login/` due to missing `authtoken_token` table.

## Changes

- **`vercel.json`** ŌĆö Add `"includeFiles": "staticfiles/**"` to the `@vercel/python` build config so collected assets are packaged into the Lambda; bump `maxLambdaSize` from 15 mb ŌåÆ 30 mb to accommodate them.

- **`build_files.sh`** ŌĆö Add `python manage.py migrate --noinput` before `collectstatic`; without this, the auth token table never exists in production.

- **`settings.py`** ŌĆö Switch `STATICFILES_STORAGE` from `CompressedManifestStaticFilesStorage` to `CompressedStaticFilesStorage`. The manifest variant requires `staticfiles.json` to be present and valid at startup; on serverless this is fragile. The non-manifest variant resolves files by name directly.

- **`backend/README.md`** ŌĆö Document the static file strategy and verification steps:
  ```bash
  curl -I https://<domain>/static/admin/css/base.css
  curl -I https://<domain>/static/jazzmin/css/main.css
  ```

title: Bump markdown from 3.6 to 3.8.1 in /python
body:
Bumps [markdown](https://github.com/Python-Markdown/markdown) from 3.6 to 3.8.1.
Release notes

Sourced from markdown's releases.

Release 3.8.1

Fixed

  • Ensure incomplete markup declaration in raw HTML doesn't crash parser (#1534).
  • Fixed dropped content in md_in_html (#1526).
  • Fixed HTML handling corner case that prevented some content from not being rendered (#1528).

Release 3.8

Changed

  • DRY fix in abbr extension by introducing method create_element (#1483).
  • Clean up test directory by removing some redundant tests and port non-redundant cases to the newer test framework.
  • Improved performance of the raw HTML post-processor (#1510).

Fixed

  • Backslash Unescape IDs set via attr_list on toc (#1493).
  • Ensure md_in_html processes content inside "markdown" blocks as they are parsed outside of "markdown" blocks to keep things more consistent for third-party extensions (#1503).
  • md_in_html handle tags within inline code blocks better (#1075).
  • md_in_html fix handling of one-liner block HTML handling (#1074).
  • Ensure <center> is treated like a block-level element (#1481).
  • Ensure that abbr extension respects AtomicString and does not process perceived abbreviations in these strings (#1512).
  • Ensure smarty extension correctly renders nested closing quotes (#1514).

Release 3.7

Changed

Refactor abbr Extension

A new AbbrTreeprocessor has been introduced, which replaces the now deprecated AbbrInlineProcessor. Abbreviation processing now happens after Attribute Lists, avoiding a conflict between the two extensions (#1460).

The AbbrPreprocessor class has been renamed to AbbrBlockprocessor, which better reflects what it is. AbbrPreprocessor has been deprecated.

A call to Markdown.reset() now clears all previously defined abbreviations.

Abbreviations are now sorted by length before executing AbbrTreeprocessor to ensure that multi-word abbreviations are implemented even if an abbreviation exists for one of those component words. (#1465)

Abbreviations without a definition are now ignored. This avoids applying abbr tags to text without a title value.

Added an optional glossary configuration option to the abbreviations extension.

... (truncated)

  • Backslash Unescape IDs set via attr_list on toc (#1493).
  • Ensure md_in_html processes content inside "markdown" blocks as they are parsed outside of "markdown" blocks to keep things more consistent for third-party extensions (#1503).
  • md_in_html handle tags within inline code blocks better (#1075).
  • md_in_html fix handling of one-liner block HTML handling (#1074).
  • Ensure <center> is treated like a block-level element (#1481).
  • Ensure that abbr extension respects AtomicString and does not process perceived abbreviations in these strings (#1512).
  • Ensure smarty extension correctly renders nested closing quotes (#1514).

[3.7.0] - 2024-08-16

Changed

  • Refactor abbr Extension

    A new AbbrTreeprocessor has been introduced, which replaces the now deprecated AbbrInlineProcessor. Abbreviation processing now happens after Attribute Lists, avoiding a conflict between the two extensions (#1460).

    The AbbrPreprocessor class has been renamed to AbbrBlockprocessor, which better reflects what it is. AbbrPreprocessor has been deprecated.

    A call to Markdown.reset() now clears all previously defined abbreviations.

    Abbreviations are now sorted by length before executing AbbrTreeprocessor to ensure that multi-word abbreviations are implemented even if an abbreviation exists for one of those component words. (#1465)

    Abbreviations without a definition are now ignored. This avoids applying

title: Bump requests from 2.31.0 to 2.33.0
body:
Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.31.0 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • ­¤ōŻ Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. ­¤ōŻ

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

title: Fix GA8 atlas packing and CELM envelope for macOS 13+
body:
Two bugs caused template (grayscale+alpha) images packed into atlases to
render incorrectly on macOS 14, manifesting as missing or garbled icons
in apps like Rectangle

Packer: GA8 sub-image positioning was using a fixed 4-bytes-per-pixel
stride, but vImage's deepmap2 encoder reads GA8 data at 2 bpp.
Sub-images placed at byte offset `x * 4` past the `width * 2` boundary
fell into the row padding region that vImage skipped, so most of the GA8
atlas content was never encoded.  Use the actual pixel size (2 for GA8,
4 for BGRA) for both the buffer stride and sub-image positioning

Compression envelope: Apple's actool emits CELM ver=0 (with the 16-byte
sub-header) for DMP2-compressed packed atlases when min_deploy is
macOS 13+.  We were unconditionally emitting ver=2.  CoreUI on macOS
14 decodes ver=2 GA8 atlases as kCGImageAlphaNoneSkipLast, dropping
the alpha channel entirely.  Switch to ver=0 for macOS >= 13.0; older
targets still use ver=2 to match Apple's behaviour there

title: chore: switch from conda to uv
body:
Replaces all conda-based environment management with [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) across the project.

## Changes

- **`Dockerfile`**: Swap `continuumio/miniconda3` base for `python:3.11-slim`; replace `conda create`/`conda activate`/`pip install` with `uv venv` + `uv pip install`; expose venv via `PATH` so it's always active in the container
- **`docs/Installation.md`**: Replace `conda create`/`conda activate` setup steps with `uv venv --python 3.11` + `source .venv/bin/activate`; replace `pip install` with `uv pip install`; drop the conda-forge system-deps fallback block
- **`scripts/install/interactive_blender.sh`**: Replace `$CONDA_PREFIX` with `$VIRTUAL_ENV` in the `CFLAGS` include path
- **`.github/workflows/checks.yml`**: Replace `actions/setup-python` + bare `pip install` with `astral-sh/setup-uv@v5` + `uv pip install --system`
- **`.python-version`** *(new)*: Pin `3.11` for uv/pyenv tooling

> `docs/ExportingToSimulators.md` conda references are intentionally untouched ŌĆö they reflect NVIDIA IsaacSim's own required setup, not this project's environment.

title: Bump protobuf from 3.19.3 to 5.29.6
body:
Bumps [protobuf](https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf) from 3.19.3 to 5.29.6.
Release notes

Sourced from protobuf's releases.

Protocol Buffers v34.0-rc1

Announcements

  • This version includes breaking changes to: C++, Objective-C, PHP, Python.
  • [Bazel] Remove deprecated ProtoInfo.transitive_imports. Use equivalent transitive_sources instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/0a5c2f6b633c1e5259f566cb42d30fe347b8aadb)
  • [C++] Make generator headers private (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/3a2af3510f0d454dbe3e4dc281674b61c4d20b9e)
  • [C++] Add a debug check that the target of CopyFrom is not a descendant of the source. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/7a7589823d2cfaaf7994b050e98d5d553bc9b1c1)
  • [C++] Add [[nodiscard]] to many APIs. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/a70115f33f9af2c4b2202c800b84837e7fe0d738)
  • [C++] Make the arena-enabled constructors of RepeatedField, RepeatedPtrField, and Map private. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/ef890c3d0c79398c70e047fe5dd893f460ba2336)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::label() in OSS. Use is_repeated() or is_required() instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b76faa921fdd244f374c7be0bddd4050fc42c292)
  • [C++] Removes proto2::util::MessageDifferencer::AddIgnoreCriteria that takes a raw pointer as an argument in favor of the overload that takes a unique_ptr. Remove macro PROTOBUF_FUTURE_REMOVE_ADD_IGNORE_CRITERIA (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b115358c64127896fed88b8b5ef5d91d86d8cbae)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::has_optional_keyword() in OSS. Use is_repeated() or has_presence() instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/68346ec9348e932664e58c3ecdcd1478f95233a8)
  • [C++] Remove AddUnusedImportTrackFile() and ClearUnusedImportTrackFiles(). Remove PROTOBUF_FUTURE_RENAME_ADD_UNUSED_IMPORT (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/837a2cd1d6c75402b2503ffe7cd8aeaf25868536)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::is_optional() in OSS. Use (!is_required() && !is_repeated()) instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/9dbc5d479a8e453921485d8d3de47fb3c005f1af)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated UseDeprecatedLegacyJsonFieldConflicts() (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/c301c2ca286327a21c50c0c4cd877afc9c655b00)
  • [C++] All entity names have length limit (2afb0dc)
  • [ObjC] Remove generate_minimal_imports generation option warning (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/45b1297fdaad5a9436d0e207422168c38dc45ac4)
  • [ObjC] Fix nullability annotations on some GPB*Dictionary types. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/ea67d6d26a48478a567c404679e3bb99cf230d50)
  • [ObjC] Remove -[GPBFieldDescriptor optional] (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/3414dc151eb4dcbdb2ca952e2589993bf7af75c4)
  • [Other] Remove deprecated flag for enabling MSVC support (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/97c979be6e0907e1051bee62584dac4594e73fa7)
  • [PHP] Remove deprecated PHP APIs (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/9c45014099a4f7004fab6dd1278de2f4f2a393c5)
  • [PHP] Remove deprecated PHP APIs FieldDescriptor getLabel, use IsRepeated or isRequired instead. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/42081219920c6fad17ba6ddd1e28d111bcfb3345, https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/cd76e675b14d00dda5623b30835d2bc7105fccc6, https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/42081219920c6fad17ba6ddd1e28d111bcfb3345)
  • [PHP] Add PHP typehints for setters and remove redundant GPBUtil checks (protocolbuffers/protobuf#25296) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/aee03b78929c02461a5f9d8e136a2a016359b0cd)
  • [PHP] support default values for editions/proto2 (protocolbuffers/protobuf#25161) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b01099d56350551bae3da88b97bf3027274c9f17)
  • [Python] Raise errors in OSS when assign bool to int/enum field in Python Proto. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/5b116fe2f14f49dd0cc3b76089983717f211025c)
  • [Python] Remove float_format/double_format from python proto text_format (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/e4854a186e0bfa867d5bfa5cd850608a948fd488)
  • [Python] Raise TypeError when convert non-timedelta to Duration, or convert non-datetime to Timestamp in python proto. (Original code may raise ArributeError) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/00aaca1b4d98954bc2933d7c8a5379ba6088124c)
  • [Python] Remove float_precision from python proto json_format (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/f027f1fcd52b9d080b7ee79f4024f53cf54e0dc5)
  • [Python] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::label() in OSS. Use is_repeated() or is_required() instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b76faa921fdd244f374c7be0bddd4050fc42c292)
  • [Python] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor.label (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/0a8ff55518ea5874478ad5b26515b31d186045a9)
  • [Python] Remove deprecated UseDeprecatedLegacyJsonFieldConflicts() (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/c301c2ca286327a21c50c0c4cd877afc9c655b00)
  • Protobuf News may include additional announcements or pre-announcements for upcoming changes.
  • Migration Guide may include additional guidance for breaking changes.

Bazel

  • Fix: cc_toolchain should prefer protoc when prebuilt flag is flipped. (#25168) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/8c857c3a1c6a106b0a096f1c9fa504bfaca035a9)
  • Breaking change: Remove deprecated ProtoInfo.transitive_imports. Use equivalent transitive_sources instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/0a5c2f6b633c1e5259f566cb42d30fe347b8aadb)
  • Feat(bazel): wire up prebuilt protoc toolchain (#24115) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/cc23698b486e690ea2eb873cc7596a87c74a3ba6)
  • Migrate proto_descriptor_set (#23369) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/8d4dfdd39a7a242a9ed631a6ab2192c57dd9b9c8)

Compiler

  • Ruby codegen: support generation of rbs files (#15633) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/6ebdf851ba78728f0aa145d38454ed9a316fb08d)
  • Avoid collision name problems between a message named Xyz and a direct sibling enum named XyzView (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/eba53e8f172b273d679759a72ce4250131ee3df1)
  • Generalizing and implementing ValidateFeatureSupport for both Options and Features during proto parsing (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/ed3c57114d8e2b47cca7697ddaa50c1b3762a6b0)
  • Fix a bug with custom features outside of the pb package. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/872d3ce7a4da00d7dcec33ced20cfe45235935e8)
  • Fix import option handling when include_imports isn't set. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/9ef9e80afd9bc8379d578fe67e5ab0738728c04e)
  • Fix a bug in STRICT check of namespaced enums to properly check for 'reserved 1 to max' (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/1229d4adba24c0952ab85ce96bc7b7f8a1fe6d0f)
  • Prevent accidental stripping of debug_redact options via import option. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/f58b098bffa7ca4045ef7773b09151a6af5d0c28)

C++

  • Add EnumerateEnumValues function. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/397d5d99db274b379d1384814074bf7df39d32f7)

... (truncated)

title: Modernize littlefs_tools v1.2.0
body:
## Summary

Comprehensive modernization of littlefs_tools from a single-file legacy package to a well-structured, well-tested, PEP-compliant Python package.

- **Packaging:** Migrate from `setup.py` to `pyproject.toml` (PEP 517/518), pin dependency versions, drop Python 3.8 (EOL), add `__version__` to package
- **Core rewrite:** Chunked I/O (4096-byte reads vs byte-by-byte), proper exception hierarchy instead of `exit()`, snake_case naming, type hints, docstrings, input validation, `str.removeprefix()` (3.9+)
- **Unified CLI:** New `littlefs create|list|extract` subcommand interface alongside preserved legacy `littlefs_create/list/extract` entry points
- **Testing:** 37 pytest tests covering create, list, extract, CLI, validation, round-trip, and edge cases
- **CI:** Multi-OS matrix (Ubuntu/macOS/Windows), ruff linting, `python -m build`
- **Docs:** Added CHANGELOG.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, updated README.md with new CLI and build instructions

## Test plan

- [x] `ruff check littlefs_tools/ tests/` ŌĆö all checks passed
- [x] `pytest tests/ -v` ŌĆö 37/37 passed
- [x] Unified CLI verified (`littlefs create/list/extract`)
- [x] Legacy CLI verified (`littlefs_create/list/extract`)
- [x] Legacy bash integration tests pass (`test/test.sh`, `test/test_large.sh`)
- [x] `littlefs --version` prints `1.2.0`
- [x] `python -m build` produces correct sdist + wheel
- [x] CI passes on all 3 OS x 6 Python versions

title: Set web deployment default port to 3000
body:
### Motivation
- Make the lightweight WSGI web app ready for server deployment by using a sensible default port and allowing environment overrides for host/port configuration.

### Description
- Update `web_app.py` to read `HOST` and `PORT` from the environment with defaults `HOST=0.0.0.0` and `PORT=3000`, and print the runtime URL when starting the server.
- Update the `README.md` web usage section to document the new default `0.0.0.0:3000` and show how to override via `HOST`/`PORT` environment variables.

### Testing
- Ran the test suite with `python -m pytest -q` and all tests passed (`3 passed`).
- Executed the CLI verification `python mapping_system.py --date 2026-02-20` which produced the expected output (total score 9/10) and succeeded. 
- Launched the web app with `python web_app.py` and performed a browser smoke test (automated Playwright script submitted the date and captured a screenshot) which completed successfully.

title: Migrate to pyproject.toml and add headless CMS JSON API
body:
Replaces the legacy `setup.py` with `pyproject.toml` (PEP 517/518/621) and exposes the `Page` model via a REST-style JSON API to enable headless CMS usage.

## Packaging
- `pyproject.toml` replaces `setup.py` with `setuptools>=61.0` build backend
- Correct Python classifiers: drops Python 2, adds 3.8ŌĆō3.12 and Django 4.0ŌĆō4.2
- Fixes license classifier (was incorrectly `BSD`, now `LGPLv3`)
- Bumps `django` requirement from `>=3.0.7` to `>=4.0`
- Adds `manage.py` + `test_settings.py` for the test suite

## Headless CMS API

New endpoints served under `feedly/urls.py`:

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/` | Blocks health check |
| GET | `/pages/` | List all pages |
| POST | `/pages/` | Create page (auth required) |
| GET | `/pages/<id>/` | Retrieve page |
| PUT | `/pages/<id>/` | Update page (owner only) |
| DELETE | `/pages/<id>/` | Delete page (owner only) |

```python
# Example: list pages
GET /feedly/pages/
# ŌåÆ {"pages": [{"id": 1, "name": "My Page", "content": "...", "date": "2024-..."}, ...]}

# Example: create a page (authenticated)
POST /feedly/pages/
{"name": "About", "content": "Hello world"}
# ŌåÆ 201 {"id": 2, "name": "About", "content": "Hello world", "date": "..."}
```

## Bug Fixes
- `feedly/apps.py`: `name` was `'feedly.feedly'` (no such subpackage), corrected to `'feedly'`
- `feedly/__init__.py`: removes stale `default_app_config` (deprecated Django 3.2+)
- `feedly/urls.py`: removes `from django.conf.urls import url` (removed in Django 4.0); switches to explicit view imports
- `feedly/views.py`: removes broken imports from non-existent `.feed` and `.core` modules

title: Migrate clean.py to Python 3
body:
`clean.py` used Python 2-only syntax, making it incompatible with Python 3.

## Changes

- **`raw_input` ŌåÆ `input`**: Python 3 removed `raw_input`
- **`print` statements ŌåÆ `print()` calls**: Python 3 print function syntax
- **Fix regex escape sequence**: Changed string literal to raw string (`r"..."`) to eliminate `SyntaxWarning` on `\[` in the pattern

```python
# Before
ans = raw_input("remove %s ([y]/n)? " % img)
print "all images in latex: ", len(all_used_img)
regex_string = "\\includegraphics(?:\[.*?\])?\{(.+?)\}"

# After
ans = input("remove %s ([y]/n)? " % img)
print("all images in latex: ", len(all_used_img))
regex_string = r"\\includegraphics(?:\[.*?\])?\{(.+?)\}"
```

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

title: Migrate codebase from Python 2 to Python 3
body:
Both `main.py` and `experimental.py` used Python 2-only constructs (`izip`, `print` statements, `raw_input`, unguarded `map()`). This migrates the codebase to Python 3.

### `main.py`
- Drop `izip` import; use built-in `zip`
- `print "..."` ŌåÆ `print(...)`
- `raw_input(...)` ŌåÆ `input(...)`
- `map(int, ...)` ŌåÆ `list(map(int, ...))` (map is lazy in Py3)
- Integer division: `/` ŌåÆ `//`

### `experimental.py`
- `raw_input(...)` ŌåÆ `input(...)`

### `README.md`
- Requirement updated from Python 2 ŌåÆ Python 3
- Commands updated from `python` ŌåÆ `python3`

title: Fix install failure on Intel Mac due to Python version constraints
body:
- Lower requires-python from >=3.13 to >=3.12 (no 3.13-specific features used)
- Fix install_service.sh: pass --python to uv pip install so it targets the .venv instead of falling back to a system interpreter
- Gracefully fall back to Python 3.12 if 3.13 is unavailable when creating venv

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

## Chores
* Expanded supported Python versions to include Python 3.12 alongside Python 3.13
* Improved installation robustness with automatic version fallback handling and explicit environment configuration

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

title: Add weekday Azure Logic App deployment automation for GitHub JSON export
body:
### Motivation
- Provide an automated, repeatable way to deploy the containerized `/trending` service to Azure and schedule a Logic App that commits the JSON output to a GitHub repo every weekday at 3:55 PM ET.

### Description
- Update `README_AZURE.md` with a two-step workflow and examples for deploying the Container App and the weekday Logic App. 
- Improve `deploy_azure.sh` to use script-relative paths (`SCRIPT_DIR`), make the Docker build context correct, and support update-or-create behavior for an existing Container App. 
- Add `deploy_logicapp.sh` which deploys the ARM template (`templates/logicapp_arm_template.json`) with parameters for the container URL, GitHub repo, and token. 
- Change Logic App definitions in `templates/logicapp_workflow.json` and `templates/logicapp_arm_template.json` to run `Week` recurrence on MondayŌĆōFriday at `15:55` in `Eastern Standard Time`.

### Testing
- Verified shell syntax with `bash -n deploy_azure.sh` and `bash -n deploy_logicapp.sh`, both reported no syntax errors. 
- Validated JSON templates with `python -m json.tool templates/logicapp_workflow.json` and `python -m json.tool templates/logicapp_arm_template.json`, both succeeded. 
- Attempted `az account show` to validate live Azure operations but it failed in this environment because the Azure CLI is not installed (`/bin/bash: az: command not found`).

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69c5cddeff908325a65bb66ab4e2fe99)

title: Restructure project: migrate to `src/` app, remove legacy modules, update deps and docs
body: ### Motivation - Move the codebase to a slimmer packaged layout and make the runtime launcher simpler and more robust when `fastapi`/`uvicorn` are not installed. - Reduce surface area and external dependencies by removing legacy telephony/AI helper modules and feature code that were tightly coupled to the monolith. - Bump the project Python version to a newer patch release for consistency and bugfixes by updating `.python-version` to `3.11.14`. - Add a minimal `README` describing architecture, features and run instructions for the refactored project. ### Description - Updated runtime: `.python-version` changed from `3.11.9` to `3.11.14` and `README.md` was added with architecture and run instructions. - Rewrote `main.py` to act as a lightweight launcher that detects `fastapi`/`uvicorn` via `importlib.util.find_spec`, runs `src.app` with Uvicorn when available, and falls back to running `src.qa_validation.main` for local QA simulation otherwise. - Removed large set of legacy modules and features including `agent.py`, `call_handler.py`, `audio_utils.py`, `config.py`, `exotel_client.py`, `intent.py`, `lead_manager.py`, `phrase_cache.py`, `scheduler.py`, `scraper.py`, `sheets_manager.py`, and related files to reduce complexity and dependency footprint. - Trimmed `requirements.txt` to a smaller set of direct/runtime packages (removed many Google Sheets, Groq, scheduler and other heavy deps) and added only the essentials like `python-multipart` and `pdfplumber` used by the new layout. ### Testing - No automated tests were executed as part of this change. ------ [Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_e_69d898fbde308322a099688874a25ae3) --- ## Summary by CodeRabbit ## Release Notes * **New Features** * Added WebSocket-based voice call streaming with real-time audio processing * Implemented document learning system: upload PDFs and images to augment conversational knowledge * Introduced hybrid conversation routing combining pre-scripted responses with LLM fallback * Added end-of-speech detection for natural conversation pausing * **Documentation** * Added comprehensive README documentation for v3 architecture and features * **Chores** * Updated Python version to 3.11.14 * Simplified configuration structure and removed legacy integrations * Streamlined dependencies to core components only

title: feat: Implement Streamlit frontend and complete backend test script
body:
Adds the Streamlit UI layer and a self-contained backend test script to make the news aggregation + event extraction pipeline demonstrable end-to-end.

## Backend

- **`backend/test_news_extraction.py`** ŌĆö Replaces the placeholder stub with a runnable script covering three test stages: news aggregation, event extraction, and confidence-score validation. Includes built-in sample data stubs so it produces meaningful output even without the real `app.data_ingestion` modules installed.
- **`backend/requirements.txt`** ŌĆö Populated with the full dependency set: `fastapi`, `uvicorn`, `spacy`, `feedparser`, `beautifulsoup4`, `streamlitŌēź1.28`, `plotly`, `pandas`, and supporting libraries.

## Frontend

- **`frontend/app.py`** ŌĆö Four-page Streamlit app wired to the FastAPI backend via `BACKEND_URL` (defaults to `http://localhost:8001/api/v1`):
  - ­¤ō░ **Õ«×µŚČµ¢░ķŚ╗** ŌĆö article list with hours/count sliders and keyword search
  - ­¤öŹ **õ║ŗõ╗ČńøæµÄ¦** ŌĆö extracted events filterable by type and severity, with entity display
  - ­¤ōŖ **õ╗¬ĶĪ©µØ┐** ŌĆö live metrics + Plotly charts for event-type and severity distribution
  - ŌÜÖ’ĖÅ **ń│╗ń╗¤ńŖČµĆü** ŌĆö backend connectivity check and news-source listing
- **`frontend/utils/api_client.py`** ŌĆö Thin `requests`-based client covering all relevant endpoints (`/news/sources`, `/news/ingest`, `/news/latest`, `/news/search`, `/news/events/extracted`, `/events`, `/health`) with structured error returns instead of exceptions.
- **`frontend/.streamlit/config.toml`** ŌĆö Theme and logger config.
- **`frontend/pages/__init__.py`**, **`frontend/utils/__init__.py`** ŌĆö Package inits.

```bash
# Run test script (works without backend installed)
python backend/test_news_extraction.py

# Start backend + frontend
cd backend && uvicorn app.main:app --port 8001
streamlit run frontend/app.py
```

title: Bump actions/setup-python from 5 to 6
body:
Bumps [actions/setup-python](https://github.com/actions/setup-python) from 5 to 6.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/setup-python's releases.

v6.0.0

What's Changed

Breaking Changes

  • Upgrade to node 24 by @ŌĆŗsalmanmkc in actions/setup-python#1164

Make sure your runner is on version v2.327.1 or later to ensure compatibility with this release. See Release Notes

Enhancements:

  • Add support for pip-version by @ŌĆŗpriyagupta108 in actions/setup-python#1129
  • Enhance reading from .python-version by @ŌĆŗkrystof-k in actions/setup-python#787
  • Add version parsing from Pipfile by @ŌĆŗaradkdj in actions/setup-python#1067

Bug fixes:

  • Clarify pythonLocation behaviour for PyPy and GraalPy in environment variables by @ŌĆŗaparnajyothi-y in actions/setup-python#1183

title: Implement config processing and update README
body:
This pull request introduces configuration file support for the deluge-export CLI application, allowing users to provide connection details via TOML files or environment variables. The CLI now prioritizes configuration from files and environment variables, improving usability and flexibility. Additionally, tests have been added for the new configuration logic, and the documentation has been significantly expanded.

Configuration support and enhancements:

* Added a new `config.py` module implementing the `load_config` function, which locates and parses configuration files from standard paths and supports TOML parsing for Python <3.11 via `tomli`.
* Modified `cli.py` to load connection details from configuration files and environment variables, providing fallback defaults and improved error handling for invalid ports.
* Updated dependencies in `pyproject.toml` to include `tomli` for compatibility with Python versions below 3.11.

Testing improvements:

* Added `test_config.py` with comprehensive tests for configuration loading, including cases for missing files, valid/invalid TOML, and missing `[deluge]` sections.
* Updated CLI tests to mock configuration loading, ensuring tests are isolated from actual config files.

Documentation updates:

* Expanded `README.md` with installation instructions, configuration details, environment variable support, and usage examples for listing and extracting torrents.

Other minor changes:

* Removed unused `pytest` import from `test_cli.py`.This pull request introduces configuration file support and environment variable integration for the deluge-export CLI, improving usability and flexibility. It also updates documentation and adds a dependency for TOML parsing on Python versions below 3.11.

Configuration support:

* Added a new `config.py` module that loads configuration from TOML files located at `~/.config/deluge-export/config.toml` or `~/.deluge-export.toml`, and exposes `DEFAULT_CONFIG` for use throughout the application.
* Updated `cli.py` so that CLI options for host, port, user, and password default to values from `DEFAULT_CONFIG`, and support environment variables (`DELUGE_HOST`, `DELUGE_PORT`, `DELUGE_USER`, `DELUGE_PASSWORD`).

Documentation:

* Expanded the `README.md` to include installation instructions, configuration file details, environment variable support, and usage examples.

Dependency management:

* Added the `tomli` dependency for TOML parsing on Python versions below 3.11 in `pyproject.toml`.

title: feat: migrate stock news agent to support Tavily search alongside DuckDuckGo
body:
## Summary

- Added Tavily as a configurable, parallel search tool in the Stock News Agent
- When `TAVILY_API_KEY` is set, `TavilyTools` from `agno.tools.tavily` is appended to the agent's tool list alongside the existing `DuckDuckGoTools`, `WikipediaTools`, and `scrape_url`
- Agent continues to work without `TAVILY_API_KEY` (graceful fallback to existing providers)

## Files changed

- `controllers/stockNews.py` ŌĆö Conditionally import and instantiate `TavilyTools` when `TAVILY_API_KEY` is present
- `requirements.txt` ŌĆö Added `tavily-python>=0.5.0`
- `pyproject.toml` ŌĆö Added `tavily-python>=0.5.0`
- `.env.example` ŌĆö Added `TAVILY_API_KEY` placeholder

## Dependency changes

- Added `tavily-python>=0.5.0` to `requirements.txt` and `pyproject.toml`

## Environment variable changes

- Added `TAVILY_API_KEY` to `.env.example` (optional; agent works without it)

## Notes for reviewers

- This is an **additive** migration: no existing functionality is removed or changed
- The `TavilyTools` integration uses the Agno framework's built-in Tavily adapter (`agno.tools.tavily`)
- Advanced search depth and 8000 max tokens are configured for high-quality financial news retrieval


### Automated Review

- Passed after 1 attempt(s)
- Final review: The migration correctly adds Tavily as an optional, additive search provider in the Stock News Agent. The implementation is clean and consistent with the pattern established by the prerequisite financial-analysis-agent-search unit. All four reported files are modified, the conditional import pattern is sound, existing tools are preserved, and the agent falls back gracefully when no TAVILY_API_KEY is set. One minor inconsistency: `tavily-python>=0.5.0` uses a range specifier while the rest of requirements.txt uses pinned versions, but this matches how the prerequisite unit added the same dependency.

title: feat: Transform into full-stack interactive dashboard with React Force-Graph and FastAPI
body:
## Overview
This PR transforms the `agentic-knowledge-web` project from basic CLI scripts into a complete, interactive full-stack web application. It introduces a FastAPI backend layer to expose agent capabilities as REST endpoints and a modern React (Vite) frontend featuring a dynamic force-directed knowledge graph visualization.

## Key Changes

### 1. Backend (FastAPI)
- **API Layer**: Implemented full ASGI FastAPI application in `main.py` overriding the empty placeholder.
- **Routers**: Added modular REST endpoints under `src/api/` for `/chat`, `/ingest`, `/graph`, and `/reset`.
- **Dependency Injection**: Added `deps.py` for efficient Neo4j, Chroma, and LLM singleton management.
- **Graph Queries**: Enhanced `Neo4jClient` with frontend-ready graph serialization (`get_graph_data` & `expand_node`).
- **Resilience**: Added error handling around vector retrieval to gracefully handle Ollama `NaN` generation timeouts, allowing fallback to pure Graph search.
- **Model Config**: Switched local embedding model to `nomic-embed-text`.

### 2. Frontend (React + Vite)
- **Initialization**: Bootstrapped a new React + Vite project under `frontend/`.
- **Styling**: Configured deep dark themes, glassmorphism UI, and gradient components using Tailwind CSS v3.
- **State Management**: Implemented `Zustand` (`useStore.js`) for managing deeply nested graph and chat states globally.
- **Interactive Knowledge Web**: Integrated `react-force-graph-2d` to visualize RAG components.
  - **Dynamic Expansion**: Users can now right-click nodes to pull and merge localized neighbors incrementally.
  - **Auto Interaction**: Clicking nodes displays context data along with quick "Ask AI" integrations.
- **Integrations**: Fully wired up Markdown-supported chat window and drag-and-drop ingestion endpoints.

### 3. Build & Docs
- **Dependencies**: Updated `pyproject.toml` and lock files incorporating `fastapi` and `uvicorn`. 
- **Doc Rewrite**: Refactored `README.md` to reflect the new dual-server (FastAPI + Vite) startup flow and latest architectural diagram.

## How to Test
1. Start backend: `uv run python -m uvicorn main:app --reload`
2. Start frontend: `cd frontend && npm run dev`
3. Hit `http://localhost:5173`.
4. Drop a markdown file onto the bottom-left uploader to trigger knowledge ingestion.
5. Watch the Graph automatically reload, zoom across entities, right-click to expand edges, and interact with the Agent via chat.

## Notes
- **Breaking Change**: The embedding model was swapped to `nomic-embed-text` (dim: 768). If migrating an existing local environment, please press the danger "Reset" button on the UI (or manually delete `chroma_db/`) to wipe old 1024-dim `bge-m3` vectors.

title: Bump requests from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0
body:
Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • ­¤ōŻ Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. ­¤ōŻ

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

title: Direct ADB backup/restore tool for VMOS Pro cloud devices
body:
Adds a standalone backup/restore tool that operates entirely via direct ADB-over-TCP/IP connections ŌĆö no VMOS API calls involved. Backs up and restores system identity, network proxies, apps with data, accounts/logins, and contacts. Each component is independently backupable and restorable. Supports cross-owner device access via HMAC-SHA256 signed permission tokens.

### New module: `vmospro_controller/backup/`

- **`adb_connector.py`** ŌĆö ADB TCP/IP connection manager wrapping `adb connect/shell/pull/push` via subprocess
- **`direct_backup.py`** ŌĆö Connects to device, reads properties via `getprop`, pulls APKs and data archives locally via `adb pull`
- **`direct_restore.py`** ŌĆö Pushes APKs/data back via `adb push`, sets properties via `setprop`, fixes ownership/permissions
- **`permission.py`** ŌĆö Offline cross-owner token system: owner signs a `PermissionGrant` with shared secret, grantee verifies before accessing device
- **`models.py`** ŌĆö `BackupManifest`, `BackupComponent` enum, property categorization sets

### Backup components (each independent)

| Component | What's captured | Method |
|---|---|---|
| `system_identity` | All `ro.*`/`persist.*` props (IMEI, Android ID, fingerprint, etc.) | `getprop` parsing |
| `network_proxies` | Proxy mode/type/data, DNS, bypass rules | Property subset |
| `installed_apps` | Third-party APKs | `pm list packages -f -3` + `adb pull` |
| `app_data` | `/data/data/<pkg>` + `/sdcard/Android/data/<pkg>` | On-device `tar` + `adb pull` |
| `accounts` | `accounts.db` + `dumpsys account` metadata | Direct file pull |
| `contacts` | Contacts provider database | `tar` + `adb pull` |

### Integration

- 13 new skills in `agent/skills.py` including `grant_device_permission` for cross-owner tokens
- SKILL_CATALOG and policies updated
- CLI subcommands: `backup`, `restore`, `list-backups`, `grant-permission` ŌĆö none require API credentials

### Usage

```bash
# Full backup (direct ADB, no API)
python main.py backup 192.168.1.100 --port 5555

# Restore to any device
python main.py restore 10.0.0.50 --backup-id backup_1713000000_abc12345

# Cross-owner: owner grants access
python main.py grant-permission 192.168.1.100 \
  --owner-id alice --grantee-id bob --secret shared-key --expires-hours 48

# Grantee uses token
python main.py backup 192.168.1.100 \
  --permission-token "<token>" --permission-secret shared-key
```

```python
from vmospro_controller.backup import DirectBackup, DirectRestore

backup = DirectBackup("192.168.1.100", 5555)
manifest = backup.full_backup()

restore = DirectRestore("10.0.0.50", 5555)
restore.full_restore(manifest)
```

### Security

- Shell arguments escaped via `_shell_escape()` (single-quote safe)
- Property keys validated against `^[a-zA-Z0-9._]+$`
- Package names validated before use in shell commands
- Permission tokens require explicit shared secret (no default key)
- Property override files written via base64 encoding to avoid heredoc injection

title: Update pandas to support Python 3.13
body:
## Problem

The build fails during pandas compilation under Python 3.13.12 with the error "standard attributes in middle of decl-specifiers" in Cython-generated C++ code. This is a known incompatibility ŌĆö pandas 2.2.2 predates Python 3.13 and its generated C extensions cannot be compiled against it.

## Solution

Bumped pandas from 2.2.2 to 2.3.0 in requirements.txt. Pandas 2.3.0 is the first release with official Python 3.13 support and ships pre-built wheels for it, eliminating the source compilation step that triggered the error.

### Changes
- **Modified** `requirements.txt`

---
*Generated by [Railway](https://railway.com)*

title: Add tool to strip Python function bodies while preserving docstrings
body:
### Motivation
- Provide a lightweight utility to prepare large sets of test assets by removing implementations from real Python code while keeping docstrings intact.

### Description
- Add `tools/strip_function_bodies.py`, an AST-driven script that rewrites each function/method body to keep the original docstring (if any) and replace the remainder with `pass`, using precise source offsets to preserve formatting.
- Support processing single files or directories and a `--dry-run` mode to preview changes without writing files.
- Add `tests/test_strip_function_bodies_tool.py` with focused tests for in-memory rewriting and on-disk updates; the test imports the tool via `importlib` so it can exercise the module functions directly.

### Testing
- Ran `uv run pytest -q tests/test_strip_function_bodies_tool.py` and the added tests passed.
- Ran the full suite with `uv run pytest -q` and all project tests passed (no failures).

------

title: Port GitFive from Python to Go
body:
## Summary

- Complete rewrite of GitFive from Python to Go, producing a single static binary per platform
- All Python code, assets, and config files removed ŌĆö repository is now a pure Go project
- All 5 CLI commands implemented and verified: `login`, `user`, `email`, `emails`, `light`

## What's included

- **CLI** (cobra): `gitfive-go login|user|email|emails|light` with flags matching the original
- **Auth**: GitHub web login with full 2FA support (TOTP, device verification, GitHub Mobile)
- **API**: Multi-client GitHub API interface with automatic rate-limit switching
- **Scrapers**: Repos, social graph, organizations, commits, PEA detection (goquery)
- **Analysis**: XRAY (deep repo analysis), metamon (email impersonation), email generation, close friends, domain finder
- **Models**: Target, ContribEntry, StringSet with JSON export
- **Cross-compilation**: goreleaser config for linux/darwin/windows ├Ś amd64/arm64, `CGO_ENABLED=0`
- **Tests**: 46 tests (unit + E2E), all passing with `-race`

## Verified functionality

| Command | Test |
|---|---|
| `gitfive-go email <addr>` | Correctly resolves email ŌåÆ GitHub account |
| `gitfive-go user <name>` | XRAY finds emails, username history, near names |
| `gitfive-go user <name> --json out.json` | Full JSON export with all discovered data |
| `gitfive-go light <name>` | Quick email discovery from commit history |
| `gitfive-go login` | Interactive auth with session persistence |

## Test plan

- [x] `go test -race ./internal/... ./test/...` ŌĆö 46 tests pass
- [x] `go build ./cmd/gitfive` ŌĆö static binary builds
- [x] Manual testing of all 5 commands against real GitHub accounts

Closes #1

title: chore(deps): bump requests from 2.32.5 to 2.33.0
body:
Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.32.5 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • ­¤ōŻ Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. ­¤ōŻ

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

New Contributors

  • @ŌĆŗM0d3v1 made their first contribution in psf/requests#6865
  • @ŌĆŗaminvakil made their first contribution in psf/requests#7220
  • @ŌĆŗE8Price made their first contribution in psf/requests#6960

Full Changelog: https://github.com/psf/requests/blob/main/HISTORY.md#2330-2026-03-25
